data.name: A Guide for Dungeon Masters
entries: For over 40 years, Gary Gygax's The Temple of Elemental Evil has remained one of the most iconic and challenging dungeon crawls in Dungeons & Dragons history. However, the Temple's internal politics, recruitment strategies, and factional warfare create a tangled web of intrigue and power struggles.

This supplemental module is designed for Dungeon Masters who wish to run T1-4 for the first time or return to it after decades, armed with a clear and organized structure of the Temple's leadership and forces.

items: Leadership Organizational Chart: A breakdown of command structures and deployable forces within the Temple, detailing missions, raids, and counterattacks.
Factional Conflicts & Politics: An in-depth look at rivalries, hidden agendas, and power struggles among Temple factions.
Recruitment & Logistics: A guide to how the Temple replenishes its ranks and maintains control over its forces.
Dynamic Response Tactics: How the Temple reacts to player actions outside its walls.

entries.name: Why This Matters for Dungeon Masters

items: Tactical Depth: Understand each leader's command, their forces, and their responses to intrusions.
Factional Warfare: Uncover the rivalries shaping the Temple's inner conflicts and shifting alliances.
Dynamic Responses: Prepare intelligent counterattacks, reinforcements, and strategic operations against the players.
Intentional Omission: Static monsters (e.g., dungeon-bound creatures, traps, magical guardians) are excluded to focus only on forces that can react.
DM Tactical Planning: No matter what the adventurers do, the Temple's leaders will not sit idly by.

entries: Whether the players:

items: Raid an outpost or allied bandit camp
Stir up trouble in Nulb
Evade the Temple's pursuit

entries: The leaders of the Temple will not sit idly by. They will send raiding parties, assassins, and strike teams to hunt the adventurers down along merchant roads or in nearby towns to ensure that the Temple's operations remain undisrupted.
"The fungal queen dreams of freedom, but she forgets one truth: even a queen must bow before the Eye." - Hedrack, the Mouth of Iuz

entries.name: History of the Temple of Elemental Evil
entries: The Temple of Elemental Evil has evolved both within the game world and in real-world lore. This history integrates insights from discussions with Gary Gygax and 15 years of research by Joseph Bloch (Greyhawk Grognard), weaving together classic lore with later expansions.

entries.name: The True Origins of the Temple
entries: The Temple was more than just Zuggtmoy's stronghold; it was founded atop an ancient sanctum devoted to the Elder Elemental God (EEG).
Though still present within the Temple, the Cult of the Elder Elemental God remains unaware of Zuggtmoy's deception.
Iuz and Zuggtmoy's alliance orchestrated the Temple's second rising, masking their true goals behind false elemental worship.

entries.name: The Hidden 5th Level: "Beneath the Temple of Elemental Evil"
entries: Beneath the Temple of Elemental Evil, a long-forgotten fifth level is said to hold the true essence of the Elder Elemental God.
The Cult of the Elder Elemental God seeks to unleash this being, unaware that their power is actually being granted by Zuggtmoy instead.
This was an abandoned design idea revived in the fan module "T5: Beneath the Temple of Elemental Evil".

entries.name: Q2: Web of Souls
entries: This expansion reveals Lolth's deeper involvement, as her drow agents attempt to subvert the Temple for their own sinister ends. Lareth the Beautiful's hidden agenda is revealed, showing his true allegiance to Lolth rather than Iuz or Zuggtmoy.

data.name: Cult of the Elder Elemental God

entries.name: Organization & Role in the Temple
entries: The Cult of the Elder Elemental God (EEG) is a shadowy and fractured organization that thrives on chaos, destruction, and forbidden knowledge. The Cult of the Elder Elemental God (EEG) is a twisted and secretive sect devoted to Rentaq, the Elder Elemental God, an ancient and malevolent entity that predates the ordered cosmos. Though the cult has existed in various forms for millennia, it has found its strongest foothold within the Temple of Elemental Evil, where its doctrines have corrupted countless followers. Despite their apparent allegiance to the Temple, most of the cultists do not realize they are being deceived. They believe they are worshiping Rentaq, but their prayers are secretly answered by Zuggtmoy, Demon Queen of Fungi, who masquerades as the EEG and grants them divine magic.

entries.name: The True Identity of Rentaq
entries: The Elder Elemental God, called Vilp-akf 'cho Rentaq, chaotic entropy and elemental power.
It was banished and shattered by Beory, the Oerth Mother, who transformed it into a column of stone, then shattered it into countless fragments, scattering its essence across the world.
These shards of Rentaq act as conduits of power, influencing mortals and attracting cultists who believe they can restore their god to full strength.

entries.name: The Elder Elemental Eyes
entries: The Elder Elemental Eyes are artifacts of immense power, fragments of Rentaq's shattered soul.
These relics were first wielded by Rentaq's ancient prophet Rentaq the Seer that coined the name.
Cult leaders seek these artifacts, the key to Rentaq's resurrection and dominion over the elements.

entries.name: The First Rising (546–569 CY)
entries: The Cult of the EEG played a crucial role in the founding of the Temple of Elemental Evil, using its elemental doctrine to recruit zealots. Zuggtmoy and Iuz manipulated the cult, directing its efforts toward their own goals. During the Battle of Emridy Meadows, the cult was driven underground, but many survived in secrecy, awaiting the Temple's second rising.

entries.name: The Second Rising (574–Present, 579 CY)
entries: With the Temple rebuilt, the Cult of the EEG has resurfaced, continuing its deception and recruitment efforts. Many of the Elemental Temple leaders are unknowingly serving Zuggtmoy, believing they are empowering the Elder Elemental God. The cult's new objective is to unearth the lost Elder Elemental Eyes and activate the ancient Nexus beneath the Temple, hoping to reconnect with their imprisoned god.

entries.name: The Elemental Sects
entries: The four Elemental Factions (Earth, Air, Fire, and Water) are rooted in the beliefs of the EEG, though each is deceived by Zuggtmoy.
Romag (Earth Temple) and Belsornig (Water Temple) have strongest ties to the cult's doctrine.
The Elemental Nodes within the Temple serve as power nexuses for the Elder Elemental God's lingering influence.

entries.name: Zuggtmoy's Grand Deception
entries: Zuggtmoy has completely usurped the cult's worship, feeding off its energies while granting spells in the EEG's name.
The most devout cultists are still unaware, blindly serving a false god while believing they are working toward Elder the Elemental God's return.
Only a few high-ranking priests within the cult suspect the truth, but even they are unsure how much of their power still stems from the EEG itself.

entries.name: Iuz's Interference
entries: Iuz, a rival demigod, uses the cult for his own ends, directing its resources and manpower into furthering his conquest of the Flanaess.
Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Temple, enforces Iuz's will, ensuring that cultists remain loyal to him above all else.
The cult is fractured, with many followers torn between loyalty to Iuz, devotion to Zuggtmoy, and faith in the Elder Elemental God.

items: Locate the Elder Elemental Shards: The cult believes these artifacts will allow them to restore a portion of Rentaq's power.
Expand Control Over the Elemental Nodes: They seek to dominate the Nodes beneath the Temple, using them to channel the Elder Elemental God's influence into Oerth.
Destroy Opposing Factions: The cult sees Lolth, Iuz, and even Zuggtmoy as obstacles to their true god's return and works in secret to undermine them.
Free Rentaq from Imprisonment: By gathering the shattered essence of the Elder Elemental God, they hope to fully resurrect him.
Trigger the Elemental Apocalypse: The cult preaches that the world must be consumed by elemental chaos, wiping out civilization and returning the world to its primal state.

data.name: Leadership of the Temple of Elemental Evil
entries: The Temple of Elemental Evil seethes with intrigue and power struggles, its factions locked in a relentless battle for dominance beneath the shadow of greater dark forces. At the heart of this sinister organization are powerful leaders driven by ambition, each serving their own goals while paying lip service to the greater cause. The Temple's leadership is a tangled web of deception and ambition, with allegiances split among Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi; Iuz, the Old One; and the distant, insidious schemes of Lolth, the Spider Queen.

entries.name: The Sinister Hierarchy of Chaos and Deception

items: {@b Primary Objective:} Restore the Temple to its former power, unleash elemental chaos upon the Flanaess.
{@b Iuz's Agenda:} Expand his dominion into Furyondy and Veluna, using the Temple as a strategic foothold.
{@b Zuggtmoy's Influence:} Work toward her release from imprisonment while manipulating the elemental factions.
{@b Lolth's Intrigue:} Infiltrate and ultimately subvert the Temple for her own dark designs.
{@b Internal Struggles:} Each faction competes for dominance, seeking control over the Temple.

entries.name: The Greater Temple Leadership
entries: A handful of powerful leaders control the Temple, manipulating the elemental factions to secure their dominance. While the elemental cults remain devoted to the Cult of the Elder Elemental God, only a select few within the Greater Temple are privy to the truth: the Temple ultimately serves Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. These high-ranking individuals—Hedrack, Barkinar, Senshock, and Commander Deggum—orchestrate the cults' efforts without revealing Zuggtmoy's influence.

entries.name: Hedrack, Supreme Commander, Mouth of Iuz

items: {@b Role:} Supreme commander of the Temple, enforcing Iuz's will.
{@b Goals:} Ensure Iuz's influence grows while balancing Zuggtmoy's presence.
{@b Personality:} Ruthless, manipulative, and fanatically devoted to Iuz.
{@b Rivals:} Senshock seeks to undermine him; Barkinar positions himself as his successor.
{@b Role:} Military leader and Hedrack's heir apparent.
{@b Goals:} Position himself as the Temple's next supreme commander.
{@b Personality:} Calculating, disciplined, and ambitious.
{@b Relationships:} Works with Deggum to secure the Temple but keeps a close eye on his rivals.
{@b Role:} Arcane adviser and intelligence overseer.
{@b Goals:} Seize power from Hedrack and reshape the Temple to his vision.
{@b Personality:} Cunning, manipulative, and power-hungry.
{@b Relationships:} Pretends loyalty to Hedrack but secretly conspires with Falrinth.
{@b Role:} Master of security, counterintelligence, and Temple enforcement.
{@b Goals:} Maintain order and root out threats within and outside the Temple.
{@b Personality:} Paranoid, sadistic, and calculating.
{@b Relationships:} Works with Barkinar, distrusts Senshock and Smigmal.

entries.name: Feldrin, Commander Guards and Supply Chains

items: {@b Role:} Oversees internal security, supply chain logistics, and allied bandit operations.
{@b Goals:} Ensure the smooth flow of resources, captives, and stolen goods to the Greater Temple while maintaining his own position of influence.
{@b Personality:} Calculating, ambitious, and ruthless.
{@b Rivals:} Distrusts Senshock, sees Barkinar as a political threat, and watches Hedrack closely.

entries.name: Falrinth, Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death

items: {@b Role:} Scholar of forbidden lore and artifact guardian.
{@b Goals:} Unlock the Orb's secrets and serve his drow patrons.
{@b Personality:} Paranoid, obsessive, and secretive.
{@b Relationships:} Romantically linked to Smigmal Redhand, but secretly pursues his own agenda.

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand, Commander of the Outer Defenses

items: {@b Role:} Assassin and defender of the Temple's surface operations.
{@b Goals:} Protect the Temple while expanding her influence through espionage.
{@b Personality:} Ruthless, manipulative, and paranoid.
{@b Relationships:} Loyal to Falrinth but wary of Hedrack and Senshock.

entries.name: The Four Elemental Temples
entries: The four elemental cults—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—operate semi-independently under the Greater Temple's authority. Each is led by a high priest who vies for supremacy, seeking to prove their element's dominance over the others. The four lesser elemental temples—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—are devoted to the Elder Elemental God, believing their power stems from its boundless elemental chaos. Each faction believes their element is supreme, and through their rituals, they will eventually awaken the Elder Elemental God (EEG) to reshape the world in their image.

entries.name: The Black Earth (Earth Temple)
entries: {@b Romag (Earth Temple):} A calculating and methodical leader who values stability and secrecy above all else. He strengthens his influence in Nulb through intermediaries and spies, but he fears the Water Temple's growing power.
{@b Belief:} The Elder Elemental God embodies the foundation of existence. Only through destruction and decay can new empires rise from the ruins of the old.
{@b Goal:} Weaken Veluna and Furyondy by sabotaging agriculture, inciting earthquakes, and toppling fortifications.
{@b Relationships:} Feuds with Belsornig and Alrrem while secretly seeking Hedrack's favor.

entries.name: The Howling Hatred (Air Temple)
entries: {@b Kelno (Air Temple):} Is arrogant and fanatical, convinced that he alone speaks for Elemental Evil. He plots against the Fire and Water Temples through sabotage and subterfuge, though his influence is waning.
{@b Belief:} The Elder Elemental God manifests as an invisible storm, bringing whispers of chaos and the power to sweep away civilization.
{@b Goal:} Focuses on controlling the skies, manipulating weather, and summoning devastating winds to erode their enemies' morale.
{@b Relationships:} Distrusts Belsornig, fears Alrrem, and seeks Romag's alliance.

entries.name: The Eternal Flame (Fire Temple)
entries: {@b Alrrem (Fire Temple):} Is aggressive and ambitious, forging alliances with fire giants and other destructive forces. He dreams of a world consumed by flame, with the Temple at its heart.
{@b Belief:} The Elder Elemental God is fire incarnate, consuming all in its path and purging the weak.
{@b Goal:} Spreading destruction through arson, terror tactics, and fire-wielding warriors. They seek to burn away the old world to make way for the new.
{@b Relationships:} Holds deep grudges against Romag and Kelno, seeks Belsornig's destruction.

entries.name: The Crushing Wave (Water Temple)
entries: {@b Belsornig (Water Temple):} Is the most cunning of the four high priests, ruling through an intricate network of spies in Nulb and beyond. His ultimate ambition is to dominate the Temple—even if it means betraying the Elder Elemental God.
{@b Belief:} The Elder Elemental God resides deep within the endless seas, waiting to drown the world in an eternal deluge.
{@b Goal:} Flood the lands, sabotage trade routes, and infiltrate coastal cities with spies and saboteurs.
{@b Relationships:} Seeks to manipulate Kelno and eliminate Alrrem, while keeping Romag at bay.

entries.name: Iuz's Influence
entries: Iuz, the demigod of deceit and domination, sees the Temple of Elemental Evil as a pawn in his grand strategy of conquest. The Temple's clergy believe they serve the Elder Elemental God, but in truth, their spells come from Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. Iuz aims to subjugate Zuggtmoy while turning the Temple into a launching point for war against Furyondy, Veluna, and Verbobonc.

entries.name: Hedrack's Role
entries: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Greater Temple, is Iuz's enforcer, keeping the elemental cults in check while secretly orchestrating Zuggtmoy's release.

entries.name: Political Struggles
entries: Many clerics and warlords within the Temple bristle at Iuz's growing dominance, clinging to their faith in the Elder Elemental God—whether real or imagined. This brewing dissent forces Hedrack to tread carefully, balancing fear and diplomacy to maintain control.

entries.name: Strategic Goal
entries: Iuz's ultimate goal is to forge a portal linking the elemental nodes to his capital, Dorakaa, unleashing his armies upon the heart of the Flanaess.

entries.name: Lolth's Infiltration
entries: As Iuz commands the Temple from above, Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders, spins a web of deceit within its ranks. The Temple stands upon an ancient site sacred to the Elder Elemental God, and Lolth is determined to prevent its full resurgence. Holding the key to the Elder Elemental God's prison, she is a crucial force in deciding its fate. Unlike Iuz, who rules through brute strength and fear, Lolth prefers subterfuge, deception, and betrayal.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful
entries: A charismatic recruiter and secret agent of Lolth, luring followers into her web of intrigue.

entries.name: Falrinth
entries: A cunning wizard who serves Lolth from the shadows while feigning loyalty to Hedrack.

entries.name: Tactics
entries: Sowing discord through misinformation, eroding Iuz's grip on the Temple, and fueling internal rivalries.

entries.name: The Greater Temple
entries: The Greater Temple of the Elder Elemental God stands as the true seat of power within the Temple of Elemental Evil. While the lesser Elemental Temples—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—battle for dominance, the Greater Temple orchestrates their conflicts, ensuring they remain pawns in a grander scheme. The elemental cultists believe they labor to awaken the Elder Elemental God, but only the highest-ranking members know the truth: the Temple serves Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. Yet even this secret is clouded by an even darker force—Iuz, the Old One, who bends the Temple to his own ambitions. The Greater Temple's leadership is a web of shifting allegiances, deceit, and dark devotion, where the strongest seek to control the elemental factions while advancing their own schemes.

entries.name: Hedrack – Supreme Commander
entries: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Greater Temple, is a ruthless and calculating high priest, personally appointed by Iuz to oversee the Temple of Elemental Evil. A master of political intrigue and religious zealotry, he skillfully maintains the elemental cults' allegiance while suppressing Zuggtmoy's lingering influence. He views the Temple's success as a stepping stone to greater power under Iuz's dominion.

entries.name: Personality and Goals
entries: {@b Personality:} Commanding, ruthless, and unwavering in his devotion to Iuz.
{@b Goals:} Ensure the Temple's resurgence, maintain order of elemental factions, increase Iuz's influence
{@b Strengths:} Powerful cleric, master manipulator, feared by allies and enemies alike
{@b Weaknesses:} Underestimates rivals, views non-Iuzian cultists as expendable

{@b Rivals:} Senshock covertly works to unseat him, viewing himself as the Temple's rightful master. Barkinar positions himself as his successor. Lolth's agents seek to erode his control from the shadows.
"Power is not granted. It is taken from the bones of the weak—and I leave nothing behind." - Hedrack, Mouth of Iuz

entries.name: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Temple
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Cleric level 9, chaotic evil}
{@i Armor Class 26 (+3 plate, +3 shield)}
{@i Hit Points 67 (9d8 + 27)}
{@i Speed 30 ft.}
{@i Condition Immunities Paralyzed, Restrained (ring of free action)}
{@i Saving Throws Wis +7, Cha +6}
{@i Skills Perception +7, Religion +3}
{@i Damage Resistances Fire, Necrotic}
{@i Senses passive Perception 17}
{@i Languages Common, Giant}
{@i Challenge 6 (2,300 XP)}

entries.name: Equipment
entries: +3 Plate Armor, +3 Shield
{@item Ring of Free Action} (prevents paralysis and restraints)
Mace of Smiting or +2 Warhammer
{@item Daern's Instant Fortress}
Black Scarab (Temple Artifact)
Potions: Flying, Polymorph, Superior Healing

entries.name: Spell Casting
entries: Hedrack is a 9th-level spellcaster (Wisdom-based, spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks). Spells prepared:

items: {@b Cantrips (at will):} Light, Resistance, Sacred Flame, Spare the Dying
{@b 1st level (4 slots):} Command, Cure Wounds, Detect Magic, Inflict Wounds, Protection from Evil and Good
{@b 2nd level (3 slots):} Find Traps, Hold Person, Silence
{@b 3rd level (3 slots):} Animate Dead, Bestow Curse, Dispel Magic
{@b 4th level (3 slots):} Divination
{@b 5th level (1 slot):} Dispel Evil and Good

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Mace of Smiting.} Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit (+8 vs. constructs), reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage (or 8 (1d6 + 5) vs. constructs). Natural 20, additional 7 bludgeoning damage.
{@b +2 Warhammer.} Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage, or 9 (1d10 + 4) if wielded with two hands.
"Earth crumbles, fire fades, water dries, and air scatters. Only chaos is eternal, and only I wield the reins of that chaos."

Hedrack is the Temple of Elemental Evil's most formidable antagonist, surpassed only by Zuggtmoy herself. As Supreme Commander and Mighty Priest of Iuz, he wields immense magical and political power, ruling through fear and ruthless ambition. His influence stretches across the Temple's factions, forcing chaos into submission under his singular, terrifying vision.

entries.name: Appearance and Presence
entries: Draped in flowing black and crimson robes inscribed with the sigils of Iuz, Hedrack's commanding presence exudes an air of menace, his cold gaze capable of unsettling even the most battle-hardened warriors.

entries.name: Agent of Iuz, the Elder Elemental Eye
entries: Hedrack carefully navigates the Temple's fractured loyalties, ensuring Iuz's dominance while keeping Zuggtmoy's influence contained. He rewards obedience and eliminates dissent before it becomes a threat.
{@b Iuz's Emissary:} Sent by Iuz to oversee the Temple, Hedrack is tasked with preparing the cult's forces for conquest and securing Zuggtmoy's debt to Iuz.

{@b Zuggtmoy's Influence:} While he outwardly maintains loyalty to Zuggtmoy, he regards her imprisonment as a convenient tool to keep the temple under his control. To exploit without truly freeing her.

entries.name: Hedrack's Relationships and Allies
entries: Hedrack rules the Temple's leadership through calculated terror and ruthless alliances, ever watchful for those who dare challenge his dominion.

entries.name: Barkinar Commander of the Greater Temple Troops
entries: Hedrack promoted Barkinar to oversee the temple's growing military forces. He considers Barkinar loyal but keeps him occupied with military concerns to prevent political aspirations. Though a trusted enforcer, Hedrack ensures Barkinar remains preoccupied with warfare, preventing him from seeking greater power.

entries.name: Deggum Commander of Temple Security
entries: Hedrack has shaped Deggum into a devoted enforcer, overseeing Temple security and reporting only to him. While fiercely loyal, whispers of his ambition and rumored ties to Lolth make him a dangerous asset—one Hedrack keeps on a short leash.

entries.name: Feldrin Bandit Commander and Enforcer
entries: Feldrin leads the Temple's raiders, striking at Hedrack's enemies and securing vital supplies. Yet his mercenary heart makes his loyalty fragile—an expendable blade kept sharp for as long as he remains useful.

entries.name: Lucius Graeme Courier and Spy Master
entries: Lucius acts as a courier and spy for the Greater Temple. He weaves a web of whispers for Hedrack, ferrying secrets between the Temple's agents. His intelligence is invaluable, often allowing Hedrack to silence dissent before it grows. Yet trust is fleeting in the Temple—so long as Lucius remains useful, he lives.

entries.name: Falrinth Wizard, Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death
entries: Though Falrinth reports indirectly through Senshock, Falrinth's arcane knowledge is invaluable to the Temple, but his true loyalties are a shadowed enigma. Hedrack suspects his ties to Lolth and watches him with quiet paranoia, waiting for the moment to dispose of him—without jeopardizing the Temple's arcane power.

entries.name: Senshock Lord Wizard of the Greater Temple
entries: Senshock, the Temple's master of arcane defenses, is Hedrack's most dangerous rival. A cunning and paranoid wizard, he views himself as the true power behind the cult and longs to unseat Hedrack. Though their alliance is uneasy, Hedrack tolerates Senshock's expertise while constantly working to curb his influence.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful (Moathouse Leader)
entries: Hedrack sees Lareth as a valuable but dangerously ambitious servant of the Elder Elemental God. While he supports Lareth's efforts at the Moathouse, he remains cautious of his growing influence, unaware that Lareth is, in truth, an agent of Lolth.
"Lareth the Beautiful is the dark hope of chaotic evil: young, handsome, well endowed in abilities and aptitudes, thoroughly wicked, depraved and capricious." - Gary Gygax

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand, Shadow of the Temple
entries: Smigmal, the Temple's elusive assassin, commands the brigands in the outer ruins, yet her true loyalties lie with her lover, Falrinth. While she serves Hedrack in name, she operates in secrecy, using a hat of disguise to infiltrate and manipulate from the shadows. Hedrack tolerates her presence for now, but he watches closely, ready to eliminate her should she overstep her bounds.

entries.name: Senshock – Lord Wizard
entries: Senshock, the Temple's master of arcane defense, is a cunning and ruthless wizard who views the Temple as a tool for his own ascension. While he publicly serves Hedrack, he secretly maneuvers to become the true power behind the cult. Ensuring the Temple's magical fortifications remain impenetrable, Senshock works tirelessly to eliminate any rival who threatens his growing dominion.

entries.name: Position and Personality
entries: {@b Position:} Lord Wizard of the Temple of Elemental Evil
{@b Personality:} Coldly arrogant, ruthlessly ambitious, and deeply paranoid.
{@b Goals:} Seize ultimate power through arcane mastery, eliminate Hedrack, and reshape the Temple to his vision.
{@b Strengths:} Unmatched in magical defenses, skilled in manipulation and deception.
{@b Weaknesses:} Trusts no one, blinded by his own genius, and prone to overcomplicated schemes.

entries.name: The Master of Arcane and Deception
entries: A sinister figure draped in black and crimson robes, Senshock is a vision of arcane mastery. Silver-threaded sigils of Zuggtmoy and Iuz decorate his garments, and a distinctive scarab pendant marked 'TZGY' grants him control over the Temple's hidden mechanisms. His once-dark hair is now streaked with silver, his narrow face frozen in perpetual disdain. Behind his piercing eyes lies a mind brimming with forbidden knowledge. In his grasp, a staff crowned with a pulsating black crystal radiates dark power—a conduit of destruction and a testament to his authority.

entries.name: Daily Operations and Leadership
entries: Senshock's contributions extend beyond the battlefield; his daily routine is filled with a variety of crucial activities aimed at fortifying the temple.

items: {@b Arcane Research:} Obsessively experiments with forbidden magics, crafting spells and artifacts to increase his dominion.
{@b Oversight:} Manipulates the Temple's clergy and elemental factions, ensuring they remain divided and reliant on his counsel.
{@b Command Over Giants:} Keeps the Temple's monstrous allies—giants, trolls, and demons—in check through displays of raw magical supremacy.

entries.name: Senshock, Lord Wizard
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Wizard level 9, lawful evil}
{@i Armor Class 12 (Bracers of Defense, Ring of Protection) or 14 with Mage Armor}
{@i Hit Points 40 (9d8)}
{@i Speed 30 ft.}
{@i Saving Throws Int +7, Wis +2}
{@i Skills Arcana +7, Investigation +7}
{@i Condition Immunities Fire (via Staff of Fire)}
{@i Senses passive Perception 9}
{@i Languages Common, Giant, Goblin}
{@i Challenge 5 (1,800 XP)}

entries.name: Special Equipment
entries: Bracers of Defense (AC bonus while unarmored)
{@item Ring of Protection} (+1 AC and saving throws)
{@item Ring of Feather Falling} (Prevents fall damage)
Staff of Fire (Grants fire resistance, spellcasting)
{@item Spell Scroll of Teleport}

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: Senshock is a 9th-level spellcaster. His spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks). He has the following wizard spells prepared:

items: {@b Cantrips (at will):} Fire Bolt, Light, Mage Hand, Shocking Grasp
{@b 1st level (4 slots):} Detect Magic, Mage Armor, Magic Missile, Unseen Servant
{@b 2nd level (3 slots):} Invisibility, See Invisibility, Web
{@b 3rd level (3 slots):} Dispel Magic, Haste, Lightning Bolt
{@b 4th level (3 slots):} Charm Monster, Polymorph (×2)
{@b 5th level (1 slot):} Conjure Elemental, Teleportation Circle

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Staff of Fire.} Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d6) bludgeoning damage, or 4 (1d8) bludgeoning damage if wielded with two hands.

entries.name: History and Ambitions
entries: Senshock, Lord Wizard of the Temple of Elemental Evil, is a man consumed by ambition, forbidden knowledge, and festering resentment. Once a forgettable figure in Verbobonc's arcane circles, his hunger for power led him to Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. From a lowly potion-seller trafficking in illicit substances to a master of the Temple's dark sorceries, his path has been one of slow, deliberate corruption. Senshock's rise is fueled by simmering hatred and an unyielding drive to claim the power he believes is rightfully his.

entries.name: Early Life and Arcane Pursuits
entries: Born and raised in Verbobonc, Senshock studied magic at the H8 Ruins – a school specializing in elemental forces. He excelled in potion brewing and wand-making, but despite his skill, he remained overshadowed by his peers at the Silver Consortium, Verbobonc's premier arcane guild. Mocked for his obsessive studies of Oeridian military history, lost religions, and forgotten planar lore, Senshock grew embittered. His bruised ego led him to seek power in the forbidden, setting him on a path of corruption.

items: {@b Innate Arcane Talent:} Senshock possessed a natural gift for summoning and elemental magic, but his impatience for structured learning held him back.
{@b Scorned by Peers:} Belittled as a middling wizard, Senshock's fixation on obscure topics—Oeridian military tactics, lost religions, and forbidden planar lore—only deepened his isolation
{@b Ego-Driven Obsession:} Though outwardly composed, Senshock seethed with resentment, vowing to claim power by any means necessary.

entries.name: Defensive Role in the Greater Temple
entries: Senshock does not engage in direct combat but instead orchestrates the Temple's defenses with cold precision. His role is one of strategic oversight, ensuring the right forces are in place while intervening only when necessary. A master of arcane warfare, he is indispensable in reinforcing the Temple's magical and tactical defenses, securing its long-term survival.

items: {@b Positioning and Response:} From his vantage point behind the main altar in Area 419A, Senshock directs the battle like a master tactician. He monitors the battlefield, unleashing spells to turn the tide while ensuring he remains safely out of reach.
{@b Last Resort Tactics:} Senshock only enters the fray when victory is assured, striking down weakened foes to solidify his dominance while ensuring his own survival.

{@b Contingency Plans:} Should the Temple face defeat, he executes a precise retreat—first withdrawing to the throne room to deliver his final report to Zuggtmoy before vanishing via teleportation to a hidden sanctuary, leaving the wreckage behind.

entries.name: Hedrack (Supreme Commander)
entries: Senshock outwardly plays the role of Hedrack's trusted advisor while secretly plotting his downfall. He despises Hedrack's grip on the Temple, viewing him as an obstacle to true power. To weaken his rival, Senshock methodically sows discord among Hedrack's subordinates, exploiting their ambitions to erode his authority. By feeding Hedrack selective counsel, he ensures the Supreme Commander remains overconfident and unaware of the growing cracks in his foundation.

entries.name: Barkinar (Commander of Greater Temple Troops)
entries: Senshock regards Barkinar as a blunt instrument—useful but expendable. He keeps the warlord preoccupied with external threats by feeding him false intelligence, ensuring his focus never turns inward. While he spies on Barkinar to gauge his ambitions, he also considers the warrior-priest a potential pawn in a future coup against Hedrack.

entries.name: Deggum (Security Commander)
entries: Senshock sees Deggum's rising influence as a threat and works to keep him firmly under his thumb. He feeds the half-elf a steady stream of minor magical trinkets, fostering a sense of dependency while subtly undermining his confidence. By inundating Deggum with false reports and fabricated crises, Senshock ensures he remains too preoccupied to involve himself in the Temple's greater intrigues. If needed, Deggum may be useful in a coup against Hedrack.

entries.name: Lucius Graeme (Spy Master and Courier)
entries: Lucius Graeme is Senshock's primary informant, though the wizard suspects his loyalties are divided. To control him, Senshock carefully crafts the information he provides, ensuring Lucius remains a valuable but unwitting pawn in his greater game against Hedrack.

entries.name: Falrinth (Rival Wizard and Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death)
entries: Senshock considers Falrinth an arrogant fool, yet a dangerous one. Their rivalry is constant, with both seeking to outmaneuver the other within the Temple's arcane hierarchy. Senshock undermines Falrinth at every turn—dispatching agents to sabotage his magical experiments and spreading insidious rumors to erode his credibility among the other cult leaders.

entries.name: The Four Elemental Priests (Romag, Alrrem, Belsornig, Kelno)
entries: To Senshock, the elemental priests are useful fanatics—too blinded by their faith to see how easily they are manipulated. He maintains a delicate balance, ensuring no one faction gains dominance. By strategically granting magical aid and stoking their rivalries, he keeps them in constant conflict, rendering them too divided to pose a true threat to his own power.

entries.name: Barkinar, Commander of the Greater Temple's Troops
entries: Commander Barkinar is a master of deception and military strategy, overseeing the Greater Temple's armed forces with ruthless efficiency. Once a prominent leader of the Earth Temple, he now serves as one of Hedrack's most trusted subordinates. However, Barkinar harbors a dangerous secret—he has forsaken the Elder Elemental Eye, swearing allegiance to Iuz after a clandestine initiation into the Mysteries of the Greater Temple.

With calculated patience and unwavering ambition, Barkinar has earned Iuz's favor. Should Hedrack fall, he is the most likely successor, though whether he serves Iuz or his own ambitions remains uncertain.

entries.name: Background
entries: Barkinar's path to power began in the service of Hextor, where his hunger for dominance and wealth set him apart. Drawn by the promise of ancient secrets, he arrived at the ruins of the Temple of Elemental Evil with a band of mercenaries after the First Temple's fall. Amid the cult's internal power struggles, Barkinar proved his cunning—betraying his allies and seizing control of the Earth Temple by eliminating its former leader, Landers.

Recognizing his ambition, Hedrack personally initiated Barkinar into Iuz's secret service two years ago. Embracing his new role, Barkinar abandoned the Elder Elemental Eye and pledged himself to the Old One. Yet, ever the strategist, he maintains the illusion of loyalty to the Eye, ensuring that the lesser factions remain unaware of his true allegiance.
{@i "Hedrack is a great man, but greatness fades. When it does, I will be there to carry the banner forward."}

entries.name: Appearance and Personality
entries: Barkinar is a hardened Oeridian warrior, his presence exuding discipline and quiet menace. He wears battle-scarred, blackened plate armor emblazoned with the flaming eye of the Elder Elemental God, a symbol of both faith and authority. His short-cropped brown hair and meticulously groomed mustache contrast with the ever-present shadow of stubble from long campaigns. Every movement is measured, every word deliberate—a man who commands without raising his voice.

items: {@b Master of Manipulation:} Barkinar walks the razor's edge between loyalty and ambition, serving Iuz while maintaining the illusion of faith in the Elder Elemental Eye
{@b Strategic Enforcer:} Wields discipline and reward with precision, keeping his troops obedient while ensuring their absolute dependence on him.

data.name: Barkinar, Commander of the Greater Temple

entries.name: Appearance
entries: Deggum's gaunt frame, lank black hair, and piercing green eyes lend him an eerie, almost spectral quality. His face, perpetually shadowed with exhaustion, bears deep lines of stress and calculation. A pronounced limp does little to slow his predatory movements, making him an unsettling figure even among the Temple's hardened warriors. He wears dusky-hued studded leather armor, enchanted for both protection and silence, and carries a morningstar, crossbow, hunting knife—selected for efficiency in eliminating threats.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Deggum is a ruthless strategist who thrives in the shadows, pulling strings with quiet precision rather than overt displays of power. His unwavering loyalty to Hedrack is born from both admiration and calculation—he sees the Supreme Commander as a model of control and dominance. Unlike his more flamboyant peers, Deggum embodies cold efficiency, enforcing discipline with a careful blend of fear and necessity.

items: {@b Cold-Blooded Tactician:} Anticipates every variable, ensuring no move is made without careful calculation.
{@b Enforcer of Order:} Keeps his troops obedient through a precise balance of intimidation and calculated rewards.
{@b Manipulative Strategist:} Masters the art of deception.

entries.name: Role in the Temple of Elemental Evil
entries: Deggum oversees the Temple's security with a meticulous and ruthless approach, ensuring its defenses remain impenetrable. He coordinates patrols, fortifications, and rapid response units to neutralize intrusions. When threats arise, Deggum personally leads scouting missions, eliminating dangers before they reach the Temple's doorstep. Ever watchful, he remains the last line of defense against those who seek to dismantle the cult.

entries.name: Background
entries: Born in the shadowed groves of Welkwood, Deggum turned his back on his homeland in pursuit of forbidden power. Lareth the Beautiful, a master manipulator, saw potential in the young half-elf and whispered promises of dominance and destiny. Under Lareth's tutelage, Deggum honed his skills in deception, strategy, and coercion. His true ascent began when he brokered an alliance with the Blackthorn ogres under Chief Garghuk, proving his value to Hedrack and securing his place within the Greater Temple's inner circle.

entries.name: Key Events in His Rise to Power
entries: {@b Forsaken Heritage:} Cast aside his ties to the elves of Welkwood, choosing power over tradition.
{@b Recruitment by Lareth:} Learned the tenets of the Elder Elemental God.
{@b Ogre Pact:} Secured the allegiance of the Blackthorn ogres, delivering Chief Garghuk's warriors to the Greater Temple.
"I didn't leave Welkwood to cower in the shadows. I came to rise above those who scorned me, and I will." - Deggum

entries.name: Motivations
entries: Deggum is consumed by ambition, seeking to carve out a legacy within the Temple's hierarchy. Though fiercely loyal to Hedrack, he secretly harbors dreams of surpassing him. To solidify his power, Deggum focuses on strengthening his grip over the temple guards and ogre forces, ensuring their unwavering allegiance to him alone.

Despite his public devotion to the Elder Elemental God, Deggum knows the truth—Zuggtmoy, not the Elder Elemental Eye, holds the Temple's reins. He carefully balances his allegiance, outwardly preaching elemental destruction while privately safeguarding his own future within the cult's shifting tides of power.

entries.name: Role in the Temple of Elemental Evil
entries: Deggum commands the Temple's security with ruthless efficiency, ensuring its defenses remain impenetrable. He oversees patrols, fortifications, and rapid-response forces to neutralize intrusions before they escalate. Beyond mere defense, he leads scouting missions to monitor threats and identify potential weaknesses in enemy forces. His influence extends to the Temple's monstrous allies, whom he recruits and indoctrinates, bolstering the cult's military strength under the guise of the Elder Elemental God's prophesied awakening.

items: {@b Fortress Guardian:} Orchestrates defenses, ensuring the Temple's inner sanctum remains unbreachable.
{@b Tactical Expansion:} Strengthens Temple forces by recruiting monstrous allies and mercenaries.
{@b Espionage and Scouting:} Oversees reconnaissance operations, tracking enemy movements and preempting attacks.

entries.name: Political Relationships
entries: As commander, Deggum navigates the temple's volatile political landscape, balancing loyalty to Hedrack while countering rivals within the Greater Temple. Both Hedrack and Senshock personally mentor him, recognizing his potential for leadership. Ever diligent, Deggum carries his spellbook at all times, refining his mastery of divine and arcane magic—many of his spells mirroring Senshock's, ensuring his versatility in both faith and sorcery.

entries.name: Allies and Relationships
entries: {@b Hedrack, Supreme Commander:} One of Hedrack's most trusted subordinates, Deggum receives direct guidance from him.
{@b Senshock, High Wizard:} Deggum respects Senshock's magical prowess but wary of ambitions.
{@b Romag, Earth Temple Leader:} Maintains a tenuous alliance by supplying Romag with ogre forces.
"Let Romag and Belsornig squabble over their petty rivalries. In the end, the one who controls the gates controls the Temple." - Commander Deggum

entries.name: Rivals and Threats
entries: {@b Belsornig, Water Temple Leader:} The two compete for control over the temple's defensive resources.
{@b Commander Feldrin:} Their differing approaches to leadership often place them at odds.

entries.name: Recruitment of the Blackthorn Tribe
entries: Deggum's most valuable contribution to the temple is securing the allegiance of the Blackthorn Tribe, a brutal force of ogres, bugbears, and hobgoblins. Through the influence of Iuz and careful negotiation, he brokered a pact with Chieftain Garghuk the Bloodtooth, providing the temple with a steady stream of warriors—driven by fear rather than loyalty.

items: {@b Negotiation with Garghuk:} Maintaining a delicate balance of fear / reward to keep the chieftain in line.
{@b Deployment of Forces:} Strategically placing ogres within key positions across the temple's various levels, including the Greater Temple.
{@b Oversight of Ogre Leadership:} Managing Chieftain Gloarok in the Greater Temple and Gerard, who assists Romag in the Earth Temple.
{@b Intelligence Gathering:} Utilizing Blackthorn scouts to gather intelligence on rival factions, adventurers, and local threats around Nulb and Hommlet.

entries: "Hedrack sees potential in me, and I will not disappoint. The ogres follow because they fear me… as they should. Loyalty bought with fear and blood is the only kind that matters."

entries.name: Feldrin, of the Temple's Guard
entries: A cunning strategist and ruthless opportunist, Feldrin, a half-Oeridian, half-Flan rogue, is a master of deception, smuggling, and underhanded dealings. As commander of the Temple's brigands, bandits, and enforcers, he maintains control not through brute strength, but through guile, blackmail, and well-placed alliances. With deep criminal connections and a keen understanding of leverage, Feldrin ensures the smooth flow of stolen goods, captives, and supplies to the Greater Temple while keeping the more violent elements of his forces in line. Though he commands warriors, he is no warlord—he is a thief first and foremost, using cunning over combat, and ensuring his position remains secure through manipulation rather than direct confrontation.

entries.name: Role and Importance
entries: Feldrin commands the Temple's guards and bandit forces, responsible for both defense and supply logistics.

items: {@b Political Operative:} Trusted by Supreme Commander Hedrack, Feldrin also serves as an emissary to King Korvash Bonegnaw, ruler of the gnolls of the Jagged Fangs tribe in the Lortmils.
{@b Supply Chain:} Manages the transport of resources to the Greater Temple's operations.

entries: "Control isn't about loyalty—it's about leverage. Keep everyone hungry enough to need you, but fed enough not to bite." —Feldrin

entries.name: Feldrin's Operations
entries: Feldrin resides in Area 243a on the Temple's second dungeon level, where his lavish quarters reflect his wealth and importance. His operation relies on a structured system for securing supplies and prisoners.

items: {@b Initial Capture:} Bandits, led by Gattas Void, raid settlements near the Moathouse.
{@b Sorting & Distribution:} Loot is processed at Gefreid Patris' Hunting Cottage near Nulb.
{@b Final Transport:} Goods and captives move through a hidden tunnel into the Greater Temple.

{@b Hedrack's Trust:} Feldrin holds a degree of favor with Supreme Commander Hedrack, who relies on him for sensitive missions such as negotiating with King Korvash Bonegnaw. However, this trust is conditional—Hedrack values results, but Feldrin's self-serving nature and questionable loyalty make him a potential liability.

{@b Brunk's Ambitions:} Feldrin's half-orc lieutenant, Brunk, harbors a deep resentment toward his superior and openly covets his position. He is constantly looking for opportunities to undermine or eliminate Feldrin, provided he can do so without exposing his own treachery.
{@b Spying Missions:} Feldrin and Brunk often use disguises to spy in the local villages.

{@b Bandit Allies:} Feldrin maintains alliances with Smigmal Redhand and her bandit crew, though his self-serving nature makes his loyalty tenuous. While he recognizes their value, he sees them as tools rather than true allies.

entries: "Adventurers think themselves heroes. They're just another kind of bandit—only louder and more self-righteous." —Feldrin

entries.name: Falrinth, Master Wizard, Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death
entries: A cunning and duplicitous figure, Falrinth operates within the third level of the Temple of Elemental Evil. Though he holds no formal allegiance to the elemental temples or the Greater Temple, his scheming nature makes him a key player in the Temple's politics and intrigue. An opportunist driven by ambition, he carefully balances alliances and rivalries, ensuring his own survival while keeping his true motives concealed.

entries.name: Rogue Wizard
entries: While not an official member of the Greater Temple, Falrinth is deeply embedded in its web of deception. As an 8th-level magic-user, he operates independently, both a valuable ally and a potential threat to the leadership.
"They say the Elder Elemental God whispers in the dark. I've heard those whispers… and one day, I will whisper back."

Falrinth's connection to his quasit familiar, Kriitch, allows him to cast as a 9th-level wizard when within a one-mile radius. Though he outwardly serves Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders, his true allegiance is to power itself. He schemes to advance his own goals, betraying even Lolth if it means seizing greater control.

items: {@b Ambition:} Seeks power above all else, even if it means betraying Lolth.
{@b Duplicitous Nature:} His unpredictability makes him a potential ally or deadly foe.

entries.name: Location and Role
entries: Falrinth resides in Area 337 on the third level of the Temple, where he safeguards his secrets and resources. His chambers connect to an Escape Tunnel, a hidden route leading to the Hunting Cottage, used by bandits allied with Smigmal Redhand.
"The Nodes are not prisons; they are gateways. Those who master them will rule both this world and the next." - Falrinth

entries.name: The Orb of Golden Death
entries: Falrinth secretly possesses the Orb of Golden Death, an artifact tied to the Elder Elemental God and central to the Temple's power. He keeps the Orb hidden from his allies and superiors, viewing it as his ultimate bargaining chip or tool for personal ascension.

items: {@b Connection to the Nodes:} The Orb serves as a key to unlocking and controlling the elemental nodes.
{@b Secrecy:} Falrinth's concealment of the Orb makes him a target for both the Greater Temple and adventurers.
