{@b 2nd Level (3 slots):} Invisibility, Mirror Image, Hold Person, Levitate
{@b 3rd Level (3 slots):} Fireball, Lightning Bolt, Clairvoyance
{@b 4th Level (3 slots):} Dimension Door, Phantasmal Killer
{@b 5th Level (1 slot):} Teleportation Circle, Wall of Force
{@b Orb of Golden Death:} Allows scrying and enhances necromantic spells.
{@b Mirror of Mental Prowess:} Can detect thoughts and manipulate reflections.
{@b Tome of Leadership:} Grants Charisma bonus if studied.
{@b Vacuous Grimoire:} A cursed book that drains Intelligence.

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Dagger.} Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 4 (1d4+3) piercing damage.

entries.name: Role and Loyalties
entries: Falrinth is loosely allied with Smigmal Redhand and her bandit crew, including Commander Feldrin, who operates out of the Broken Tower. However, his true loyalty lies only with himself. Though he cooperates with these factions, Falrinth is ultimately an opportunist, always seeking the greatest personal advantage.

He also collaborates with Lareth the Beautiful, both serving as agents of Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders. Their shared mission, as dictated by Lolth, is to gain control of the elemental nodes (Nexus), though the full scope of their plan remains hidden.

entries.name: Senshock: A Cautious Superior
entries: Falrinth reports to Senshock, High Commander of the Greater Temple, but their relationship is based on mutual distrust. Senshock sees Falrinth as a useful but untrustworthy resource, aware that his loyalty to Lolth and secrecy regarding the Orb of Golden Death could threaten the Greater Temple's unity.

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand & Feldrin: Bandit Connections
entries: Smigmal Redhand: An ally of convenience, Falrinth works with her bandits, though his loyalty is tenuous at best.
Commander Feldrin: While Feldrin reports to Falrinth indirectly through Smigmal, this chain of loyalty is weak and unreliable.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful: A Dangerous Partnership
entries: Lolth's Plan: Both serve Lolth, working to manipulate the elemental nodes, possibly undermining Zuggtmoy and the Elder Elemental God.
Fragile Alliance: Despite their shared devotion to Lolth, ambition makes their alliance precarious and could lead to betrayal.
"Hedrack and his ilk think themselves untouchable, yet they dance like puppets on strings they cannot see. Strings I can cut."

entries.name: Falrinth's Tactics and Abilities
entries: A cautious strategist, Falrinth relies on magic, deception, and his quasit familiar to outmaneuver threats.

items: Kriitch the Quasit: Spies in giant centipede form, relaying information.
Escape Tunnel: Enables quick retreats or ambushes.
Preparedness: Uses divination and illusions to manipulate encounters.

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand, Commander
entries: A ruthless and cunning half-orc Fighter/Assassin, Smigmal Redhand commands the outer defenses of the Temple of Elemental Evil. Leading mercenaries and bandits from the ruined tower, she serves as the Temple's first line of defense, eliminating threats with cold efficiency. Using a {@item Hat of Disguise}, she masquerades as an elf, blending deception with assassination to manipulate both high society and adventurers who stray too close.

entries.name: Master of Deception and Deadly Precision
entries: Smigmal is an imposing six-foot figure, her lean, sinewy frame hardened by years of battle and subterfuge. Her sharp, angular features and piercing yellow eyes betray her orcish blood, though few ever see her true face. Under her magical elven guise, she appears as a refined noblewoman, effortlessly infiltrating enemy ranks and social circles.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Smigmal is a cold, calculating assassin with an iron will. She believes in striking from the shadows and maintaining absolute control over her surroundings. Her loyalty is primarily to Falrinth, her lover and secret ally within the temple, but she harbors ambitions of her own. Ruthless, pragmatic, and untrusting, Smigmal excels at manipulating others to serve her interests.

items: Calculating and Ruthless: Smigmal values efficiency over morality, always seeking the most pragmatic solution.
Devoted to Falrinth: She prioritizes her relationship with the wizard over temple politics.
Paranoia and Mistrust: Never truly trusts her superiors, always keeping an escape route in mind.

entries: "Hedrack plays the king, Senshock schemes for the throne, and Barkinar waits like a vulture. I simply watch and sharpen my knives." - Smigmal

entries.name: Smigmal's Background
entries: Smigmal hails from the war-torn Bandit Kingdoms, where survival meant mastering cunning and violence. Her lethal skills caught the attention of Temple agents, leading to her swift rise through the ranks by eliminating threats and proving herself to Supreme Commander Hedrack and Senshock.

items: Mercenary Roots: Raised among cutthroats and raiders, she learned to fight, deceive, and survive.
Temple Recruitment: Started as a scout, later placed in command of the outer defenses.
Elven Disguise: Uses a magical hat to pose as an elven diplomat, gathering intelligence in Nulb and Hommlet.

entries.name: Role in the Temple
entries: As leader of the Temple's surface defenses, Smigmal commands raiding parties and ensures no enemy infiltrates the ruined tower stronghold.

items: Lead Defensive Operations: Orchestrates raids and ambushes against Temple enemies.
Espionage and Sabotage: Works with Falrinth to spy on enemies and rivals.
Mercenary Control: Manages Temple-hired forces, ensuring loyalty through fear.

entries: "Obedience comes not from loyalty, but from fear. And fear is a currency I deal in generously." - Smigmal

entries.name: Falrinth (Lover and Wizard Ally)
entries: Smigmal's closest ally and lover, Falrinth, uses her position to gather intelligence and eliminate rivals. His quasit familiar provides her with a steady flow of spying reports from Nulb, the Temple, and beyond.

However, Falrinth's ambitions outweigh his loyalty. He sees Smigmal as a pawn in his quest for power, hoping to seize control after Hedrack's fall. Smigmal, aware of his grandiose dreams, knows he will abandon the Temple if it collapses—and she plans accordingly.

entries.name: Hedrack (Supreme Commander)
entries: Hedrack tolerates Smigmal due to her effectiveness, but distrusts her closeness to Falrinth, whose loyalty to Lolth makes him a wildcard. He keeps a watchful eye on her, using her to maintain order but never fully trusting her.

entries.name: Deggum (Commander of Security)
entries: As her direct superior, Deggum relies on Smigmal for defense reports and coordination. Though she respects his tactical mind, she sees him as a roadblock to her ambitions and remains wary of his authority.
"Barkinar may wear the armor, but it's his boots that tell the truth—always pointed toward the exit. He's waiting, watching, but when the time comes, he'll run." - Smigmal

entries.name: Faith and Ambition
entries: Smigmal, like many in the temple, pays lip service to the Elder Elemental God but does not hold true faith. She sees the cult's chaos as a useful distraction for her own agendas but has no desire to awaken the ancient power.
"Hedrack plays the king, Senshock schemes for the throne, and Barkinar waits like a vulture. I simply watch and sharpen my knives." - Smigmal

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand
entries: {@i Medium Humanoid (Half-Orc), Neutral Evil}
{@i Armor Class 19 (+1 Leather Armor, +2 Shield)}
{@i Hit Points 52 (7d8+21)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Savings Throws Dex +6, Int +5}
{@i Skills Acrobatics +9, Deception +5, Intimidation +5, Stealth +9, Sleight of Hand +6}
{@i Senses passive Perception 8}
{@i Languages Common, Orc}
{@i Challenge 5 (1,800 XP)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Assassinate.} During her first turn, Smigmal has advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn. Any hit she scores against a surprised creature is a critical hit.
{@b Evasion.} When Smigmal is subjected to an effect that allows her to make a Dexterity saving throw for half damage, she instead takes no damage on a success and half damage on a failure.
{@b Relentless Endurance (1/Day).} When Smigmal is reduced to 0 hit points, instead drops to 1 hp, unless she is killed outright.
{@b Savage Attacks.} When Smigmal scores a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, she can roll one additional weapon damage die and add it to the extra damage.

{@b Sneak Attack (1/Turn).} Smigmal deals an extra 14 (4d6) damage when she hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally and she doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Multiattack.} Smigmal makes two melee attacks.
{@b +1 Shortsword.} Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d6+4) piercing damage.
{@b Hand Crossbow.} Ranged Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6+3) piercing damage.

{@b Poisoned Blade (Recharge 5-6).} Smigmal coats her blade with an insinuative poison (Type B). The next creature she hits must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 25 poison damage over 2 rounds on a failure, or half on a success.

entries.name: Equipment
entries: +1 Leather Armor, +2 Shield, +1 Shortsword, Hand Crossbow with 10 Bolts, {@item Ring of Invisibility}, Jeweled Belt (3,200 gp), Earrings (400 gp each), Secret Compartment (30 Bloodstones, 2 Pieces of Jewelry, 50 pp in a hidden belt pocket)

data.name: Lucius Graeme, Temple Agent Courier
entries: Lucius Graeme, a Oeridian/Suel operative, commands attention—not through stature, but through an aura of quiet menace. His portly frame, draped in simple gray garments, conceals a calculating and dangerous mind. A small gray hat often shadows his scarred face, marked by an old injury from falling debris. His golden, asymmetrical eyes seem to uncover secrets no one wishes to reveal, and his smooth, unsettling voice has a way of disarming or unnerving listeners.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Once a vain and cruel nobleman, Lucius's fall from grace left him with a bitter hatred for order and morality. Now, he is a manipulative schemer, wielding intelligence and deception like a blade. Beneath his calm exterior simmers a barely controlled rage, which he unleashes without mercy when provoked.

items: {@b Master Manipulator}: Feigns weakness, allowing enemies to underestimate him.
{@b Chaos as Power}: Believes disorder breeds opportunity, shaping the world in his image.
{@b Devotion to Elemental Forces}: Sees the primordial powers of the elemental planes as neither good nor evil—only tools for the strong.

entries: "Chaos is the truest form of control—those who embrace it will shape the world to their will." - Aldren

entries.name: The Art of Deception
entries: Lucius delights in playing the fool, allowing others to underestimate him while he quietly gathers intelligence and consolidates power. He thrives in chaos, seeing it as the ultimate tool of control. To him, the elemental planes are not moral forces, but raw engines of destruction and renewal, waiting to be harnessed by those strong enough to command them.
"Civilization is a fragile mask over the face of the elemental chaos. It's a shame when it breaks… or a delight." - Lucius Graeme

entries.name: Lucius Graeme
entries: {@i Human Cleric Level 7, Neutral evil}
{@i Armor Class 16 (chain shirt, shield)}
{@i Hit Points 45 (7d8-7)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Savings Throws Wisdom +6, Charisma +5}
{@i Skills Deception +5, Insight +6, Persuasion +5, Religion +5}
{@i Senses passive Perception 13}
{@i Languages Common, Abyssal, Gnome}
{@i Challenge 5 (1800 exp)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Dark Devotion (Cleric of Evil).} Lucius has advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.
{@b Fanatical Conviction.} When Lucius is reduced to 0 hit points, he can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a success, he drops to 1 hit point instead.
{@b Spell Casting.} Spells Prepared (Spell Save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks).

items: Cantrips (at will): {@spell Thaumaturgy}, {@spell Sacred Flame}, {@spell Spare the Dying}
1st level (4 slots): {@spell Command} (×3), {@spell Cause Light Wounds}, {@spell Detect Magic}, {@spell Protection from Good}
2nd level (3 slots): {@spell Detect Charm} (×2), {@spell Find Traps}, {@spell Hold Person} (×2), {@spell Silence} (15 ft. radius)
3rd level (2 slots): {@spell Animate Dead}, {@spell Continual Darkness}, {@spell Dispel Magic}, {@spell Bestow Curse}
4th level (1 slot): {@spell Control Water} (Elemental-themed control over the Elder Elemental God's power)

entries: {@b Divination.} Used to communicate with the EEG (Zyggmoy).

{@b Amulet of the Shrouded One.} Lucius's alignment and location cannot be detected by divination magic ({@spell Detect Evil and Good}, {@spell Commune}, {@spell Divination}, {@spell Scrying}, etc.). He cannot be tracked by nonmagical means unless he allows it. Once per long rest, can cast {@spell Nondetection} on himself without expending a spell slot.

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Mace of the Withering.} Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d6+4) bludgeoning damage. The target must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or suffer 1 level of exhaustion. If the target is frightened, the attack deals an additional {@dice 1d6} necrotic damage.
{@b Unholy Smite (1/Day).} When Lucius hits with a melee attack, he deals an additional 14 ({@dice 4d6}) necrotic damage.

{@b Cloak of the Forsaken Veil (1/Day).} Lucius can cast {@spell Pass Without Trace} on himself without expending a spell slot. He also has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks while wearing cloak.

data.name: The Four Elemental Temples
entries: The four elemental cults—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—operate semi-independently under the Greater Temple's authority. Each is led by a high priest who vies for supremacy, seeking to prove their element's dominance over the others. The four lesser elemental temples—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—are devoted to the Elder Elemental God, believing their power stems from its boundless elemental chaos. Each faction believes their element is supreme, their rituals, they will eventually awaken (EEG) to reshape the world in their image.

entries.name: The Black Earth (Earth Temple)
entries: Romag (Earth Temple) is a calculating and methodical leader who values stability and secrecy above all else. He strengthens his influence in Nulb through intermediaries and spies, but he fears the Water Temple's growing power.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God embodies the foundation of existence. Only through destruction and decay can new empires rise from the ruins of the old.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Weaken Veluna and Furyondy by sabotaging agriculture, inciting earthquakes, and toppling fortifications.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Feuds with Belsornig and Alrrem while secretly seeking Hedrack's favor.

entries.name: The Howling Hatred (Air Temple)
entries: Kelno (Air Temple) is arrogant and fanatical, convinced that he alone speaks for Elemental Evil. He plots against the Fire and Water Temples through sabotage and subterfuge, though his influence is waning.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God manifests as an invisible storm, bringing whispers of chaos and the power to sweep away civilization.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Focuses on controlling the skies, manipulating weather, and summoning devastating winds to erode their enemies' morale.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Distrusts Belsornig, fears Alrrem, and seeks Romag's alliance.

entries.name: The Eternal Flame (Fire Temple)
entries: Alrrem (Fire Temple) is aggressive and ambitious, forging alliances with fire giants and other destructive forces. He dreams of a world consumed by flame, with the Temple at its heart.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God is fire incarnate, consuming all in its path and purging the weak.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Spreading destruction through arson, terror tactics, and fire-wielding warriors. They seek to burn away the old world to make way for the new.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Holds deep grudges against Romag and Kelno, seeks Belsornig's destruction.

entries.name: The Crushing Wave (Water Temple)
entries: Belsornig (Water Temple) is the most cunning of the four high priests, ruling through an intricate network of spies in Nulb and beyond. His ultimate ambition is to dominate the Temple—even if it means betraying the Elder Elemental God.

entries.name: Belief
entries: The Elder Elemental God resides deep within the endless seas, waiting to drown the world in an eternal deluge.

entries.name: Goal
entries: Flood the lands, sabotage trade routes, and infiltrate coastal cities with spies and saboteurs.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Seeks to manipulate Kelno and eliminate Alrrem, while keeping Romag at bay.

entries.name: Iuz's Influence
entries: Iuz, the demigod of deceit and domination, sees the Temple of Elemental Evil as a pawn in his grand strategy of conquest. The Temple's clergy believe they serve the Elder Elemental God, but in truth, their spells come from Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. Iuz aims to subjugate Zuggtmoy while turning the Temple into a launching point for war against Furyondy, Veluna, and Verbobonc.

entries.name: Hedrack's Role
entries: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Greater Temple, is Iuz's enforcer, keeping the elemental cults in check while secretly orchestrating Zuggtmoy's release.

entries.name: Political Struggles
entries: Many clerics and warlords within the Temple bristle at Iuz's growing dominance, clinging to their faith in the Elder Elemental God—whether real or imagined. This brewing dissent forces Hedrack to tread carefully, balancing fear and diplomacy to maintain control.

entries.name: Strategic Goal
entries: Iuz's ultimate goal is to forge a portal linking the elemental nodes to his capital, Dorakaa, unleashing his armies upon the heart of the Flanaess.

entries.name: Lolth's Infiltration
entries: As Iuz commands the Temple from above, Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders, spins a web of deceit within its ranks. The Temple stands upon an ancient site sacred to the Elder Elemental God, and Lolth is determined to prevent its full resurgence. Holding the key to the Elder Elemental God's prison, she is a crucial force in deciding its fate. Unlike Iuz, who rules through brute strength and fear, Lolth prefers subterfuge, deception, and betrayal.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful
entries: A charismatic recruiter and secret agent of Lolth, luring followers into her web of intrigue.

entries.name: Falrinth
entries: A cunning wizard who serves Lolth from the shadows while feigning loyalty to Hedrack.

entries.name: Tactics
entries: Sowing discord through misinformation, eroding Iuz's grip on the Temple, and fueling internal rivalries.

data.name: Romag, High Priest, of the Earth Temple

entries.name: The Greater Temple
entries: The Greater Temple of the Elder Elemental God stands as the true seat of power within the Temple of Elemental Evil. While the lesser Elemental Temples—Earth, Air, Fire, and Water—battle for dominance, the Greater Temple orchestrates their conflicts, ensuring they remain pawns in a grander scheme. The elemental cultists believe they labor to awaken the Elder Elemental God, but only the highest-ranking members know the truth: the Temple serves Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. Yet even this secret is clouded by an even darker force—Iuz, the Old One, who bends the Temple to his own ambitions. The Greater Temple's leadership is a web of shifting allegiances, deceit, and dark devotion, where the strongest seek to control the elemental factions while advancing their own schemes.

entries.name: Hedrack – Supreme Commander
entries: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Greater Temple, is a ruthless and calculating high priest, personally appointed by Iuz to oversee the Temple of Elemental Evil. A master of political intrigue and religious zealotry, he skillfully maintains the elemental cults' allegiance while suppressing Zuggtmoy's lingering influence. He views the Temple's success as a stepping stone to greater power under Iuz's dominion.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Commanding, ruthless, and unwavering in his devotion to Iuz.

entries.name: Goals
entries: Ensure the Temple's resurgence, maintain order of elemental factions, increase Iuz's influence

entries.name: Strengths
entries: Powerful cleric, master manipulator, feared by allies and enemies alike

entries.name: Weaknesses
entries: Underestimates rivals, views non-Iuzian cultists as expendable

entries.name: Rivals
entries: Senshock covertly works to unseat him, viewing himself as the Temple's rightful master. Barkinar positions himself as his successor. Lolth's agents seek to erode his control from the shadows.
"Power is not granted. It is taken from the bones of the weak—and I leave nothing behind." - Hedrack, Mouth of Iuz

entries.name: Hedrack, Supreme Commander of the Temple
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Cleric level 9, chaotic evil}
{@i Armor Class 26 (+3 plate, +3 shield)}
{@i Hit Points 67 (9d8 + 27)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Condition Immunities Paralyzed, Restrained (ring of free action)}
{@i Saving Throws Wis +7, Cha +6}
{@i Skills Perception +7, Religion +3}
{@i Damage Resistances Fire, Necrotic}
{@i Senses passive Perception 17}
{@i Languages Common, Giant}
{@i Challenge 6 (2,300 XP)}

Equipment: +3 Plate Armor, +3 Shield, Ring of Free Action (prevents paralysis and restraints), Mace of Smiting or +2 Warhammer, Daern's Instant Fortress, Black Scarab (Temple Artifact), Potions: Flying, Polymorph, Superior Healing
Spell Casting. Hedrack is a 9th-level spellcaster (Wisdom-based, spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks). Spells prepared:

items: Cantrips (at will): {@spell Light}, {@spell Resistance}, {@spell Sacred Flame}, {@spell Spare}
1st level (4 slots): {@spell Command}, {@spell Cure Wounds}, {@spell Detect Magic}, {@spell Inflict Wounds}, {@spell Protection from Evil and Good}
2nd level (3 slots): {@spell Find Traps}, {@spell Hold Person}, {@spell Silence}
3rd level (3 slots): {@spell Animate Dead}, {@spell Bestow Curse}, {@spell Dispel Magic}
4th level (3 slots): {@spell Divination}
5th level (1 slot): {@spell Dispel Evil and Good}

entries: Actions:

{@i Mace of Smiting. Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit (+8 vs. constructs), reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 6 (1d6 + 3) bludgeoning damage (or 8 [1d6 + 5] vs. constructs). Natural 20, additional 7 bludgeoning damage.}
{@i +2 Warhammer. Melee Weapon Attack: +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 8 (1d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage, or 9 (1d10 + 4) if wielded with two hands.}
"Earth crumbles, fire fades, water dries, and air scatters. Only chaos is eternal, and only I wield the reins of that chaos."

Hedrack is the Temple of Elemental Evil's most formidable antagonist, surpassed only by Zuggtmoy herself. As Supreme Commander and Mighty Priest of Iuz, he wields immense magical and political power, ruling through fear and ruthless ambition. His influence stretches across the Temple's factions, forcing chaos into submission under his singular, terrifying vision.

entries.name: Appearance and Presence
entries: Draped in flowing black and crimson robes inscribed with the sigils of Iuz, Hedrack's commanding presence exudes an air of menace, his cold gaze capable of unsettling even the most battle-hardened warriors.

entries.name: Agent of Iuz, the Elder Elemental Eye
entries: Hedrack carefully navigates the Temple's fractured loyalties, ensuring Iuz's dominance while keeping Zuggtmoy's influence contained. He rewards obedience and eliminates dissent before it becomes a threat.

items: Iuz's Emissary: Sent by Iuz to oversee the Temple, Hedrack is tasked with preparing the cult's forces for conquest and securing Zuggtmoy's debt to Iuz.
Zuggtmoy's Influence: While he outwardly maintains loyalty to Zuggtmoy, he regards her imprisonment as a convenient tool to keep the temple under his control. To exploit without truly freeing her.

entries.name: Hedrack's Relationships and Allies
entries: Hedrack rules the Temple's leadership through calculated terror and ruthless alliances, ever watchful for those who dare challenge his dominion.

entries.name: Barkinar – Commander of the Greater Temple Troops
entries: Hedrack promoted Barkinar to oversee the temple's growing military forces. He considers Barkinar loyal but keeps him occupied with military concerns to prevent political aspirations. Though a trusted enforcer, Hedrack ensures Barkinar remains preoccupied with warfare, preventing him from seeking greater power.

entries.name: Deggum – Commander of Temple Security
entries: Hedrack has shaped Deggum into a devoted enforcer, overseeing Temple security and reporting only to him. While fiercely loyal, whispers of his ambition and rumored ties to Lolth make him a dangerous asset—one Hedrack keeps on a short leash.

entries.name: Feldrin – Bandit Commander and Enforcer
entries: Feldrin leads the Temple's raiders, striking at Hedrack's enemies and securing vital supplies. Yet his mercenary heart makes his loyalty fragile—an expendable blade kept sharp for as long as he remains useful.

entries.name: Lucius Graeme – Courier and Spy Master
entries: Lucius acts as a courier and spy for the Greater Temple. He weaves a web of whispers for Hedrack, ferrying secrets between the Temple's agents. His intelligence is invaluable, often allowing Hedrack to silence dissent before it grows. Yet trust is fleeting in the Temple—so long as Lucius remains useful, he lives.

entries.name: Falrinth – Wizard, Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death
entries: Though Falrinth reports indirectly through Senshock, Falrinth's arcane knowledge is invaluable to the Temple, but his true loyalties are a shadowed enigma. Hedrack suspects his ties to Lolth and watches him with quiet paranoia, waiting for the moment to dispose of him—without jeopardizing the Temple's arcane power.

entries.name: Senshock – Lord Wizard of the Greater Temple
entries: Senshock, the Temple's master of arcane defenses, is Hedrack's most dangerous rival. A cunning and paranoid wizard, he views himself as the true power behind the cult and longs to unseat Hedrack. Though their alliance is uneasy, Hedrack tolerates Senshock's expertise while constantly working to curb his influence.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful – Moathouse Leader
entries: Hedrack sees Lareth as a valuable but dangerously ambitious servant of the Elder Elemental God. While he supports Lareth's efforts at the Moathouse, he remains cautious of his growing influence, unaware that Lareth is, in truth, an agent of Lolth.
"Lareth the Beautiful is the dark hope of chaotic evil: young, handsome, well endowed in abilities and aptitudes, thoroughly wicked, depraved and capricious." - Gary Gygax

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand – Shadow of the Temple
entries: Smigmal, the Temple's elusive assassin, commands the brigands in the outer ruins, yet her true loyalties lie with her lover, Falrinth. While she serves Hedrack in name, she operates in secrecy, using a hat of disguise to infiltrate and manipulate from the shadows. Hedrack tolerates her presence for now, but he watches closely, ready to eliminate her should she overstep her bounds.

entries.name: Senshock – Lord Wizard
entries: Senshock, the Temple's master of arcane defense, is a cunning and ruthless wizard who views the Temple as a tool for his own ascension. While he publicly serves Hedrack, he secretly maneuvers to become the true power behind the cult. Ensuring the Temple's magical fortifications remain impenetrable, Senshock works tirelessly to eliminate any rival who threatens his growing dominion.

entries.name: Position
entries: Lord Wizard of the Temple of Elemental Evil

entries.name: Personality
entries: Coldly arrogant, ruthlessly ambitious, and deeply paranoid.

entries.name: Goals
entries: Seize ultimate power through arcane mastery, eliminate Hedrack, and reshape the Temple to his vision.

entries.name: Strengths
entries: Unmatched in magical defenses, skilled in manipulation and deception.

entries.name: Weaknesses
entries: Trusts no one, blinded by his own genius, and prone to overcomplicated schemes.

entries.name: The Master of Arcane and Deception
entries: A sinister figure draped in black and crimson robes, Senshock is a vision of arcane mastery. Silver-threaded sigils of Zuggtmoy and Iuz decorate his garments, and a distinctive scarab pendant marked 'TZGY' grants him control over the Temple's hidden mechanisms. His once-dark hair is now streaked with silver, his narrow face frozen in perpetual disdain. Behind his piercing eyes lies a mind brimming with forbidden knowledge. In his grasp, a staff crowned with a pulsating black crystal radiates dark power—a conduit of destruction and a testament to his authority.

entries.name: Daily Operations and Leadership
entries: Senshock's contributions extend beyond the battlefield; his daily routine is filled with a variety of crucial activities aimed at fortifying the temple.

items: Arcane Research: Obsessively experiments with forbidden magics, crafting spells and artifacts to increase his dominion.
Oversight: Manipulates the Temple's clergy and elemental factions, ensuring they remain divided and reliant on his counsel.
Command Over Giants: Keeps the Temple's monstrous allies—giants, trolls, and demons—in check through displays of raw magical supremacy.

entries.name: Senshock, Lord Wizard
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Wizard level 9, lawful evil}
{@i Armor Class 12 (Bracers of Defense, Ring of Protection) or 14 with Mage Armor}
{@i Hit Points 40 (9d8)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Saving Throws Int +7, Wis +2}
{@i Skills Arcana +7, Investigation +7}
{@i Condition Immunities Fire (via Staff of Fire)}
{@i Senses passive Perception 9}
{@i Languages Common, Giant, Goblin}
{@i Challenge 5 (1,800 XP)}

Special Equipment: Bracers of Defense (AC bonus while unarmored), Ring of Protection (+1 AC and saving throws), Ring of Feather Falling (Prevents fall damage), Staff of Fire (Grants fire resistance, spellcasting), {@item Spell Scroll of Teleport}
Spellcasting. Senshock is a 9th-level spellcaster. His spellcasting ability is Intelligence (spell save DC 15, +7 to hit with spell attacks). He has the following wizard spells prepared:

1st level (4 slots): {@spell Detect Magic}, {@spell Mage Armor}, {@spell Magic Missile}, {@spell Unseen Servant}
2nd level (3 slots): {@spell Invisibility}, {@spell See Invisibility}, {@spell Web}

4th level (3 slots): {@spell Charm Monster}, {@spell Polymorph} (x2)
5th level (1 slot): {@spell Conjure Elemental}, {@spell Teleportation Circle}

data.name: Kelno, High Priest of the Air Temple

entries.name: History and Ambitions
entries: Senshock, Lord Wizard of the Temple of Elemental Evil, is a man consumed by ambition, forbidden knowledge, and festering resentment. Once a forgettable figure in Verbobonc's arcane circles, his hunger for power led him to Zuggtmoy, the Demon Queen of Fungi. From a lowly potion-seller trafficking in illicit substances to a master of the Temple's dark sorceries, his path has been one of slow, deliberate corruption. Senshock's rise is fueled by simmering hatred and an unyielding drive to claim the power he believes is rightfully his.

entries.name: Early Life and Arcane Pursuits
entries: Born and raised in Verbobonc, Senshock studied magic at the H8 Ruins – a school specializing in elemental forces. He excelled in potion brewing and wand-making, but despite his skill, he remained overshadowed by his peers at the Silver Consortium, Verbobonc's premier arcane guild. Mocked for his obsessive studies of Oeridian military history, lost religions, and forgotten planar lore, Senshock grew embittered. His bruised ego led him to seek power in the forbidden, setting him on a path of corruption.

items: {@b Innate Arcane Talent:} Senshock possessed a natural gift for summoning and elemental magic, but his impatience for structured learning held him back.
{@b Scorned by Peers:} Belittled as a middling wizard, Senshock's fixation on obscure topics—Oeridian military tactics, lost religions, and forbidden planar lore—only deepened his isolation
{@b Ego-Driven Obsession:} Though outwardly composed, Senshock seethed with resentment, vowing to claim power by any means necessary.

entries.name: Defensive Role in the Greater Temple
entries: Senshock does not engage in direct combat but instead orchestrates the Temple's defenses with cold precision. His role is one of strategic oversight, ensuring the right forces are in place while intervening only when necessary. A master of arcane warfare, he is indispensable in reinforcing the Temple's magical and tactical defenses, securing its long-term survival.

items: {@b Positioning and Response:} From his vantage point behind the main altar in Area 419A, Senshock directs the battle like a master tactician. He monitors the battlefield, unleashing spells to turn the tide while ensuring he remains safely out of reach.
{@b Last Resort Tactics:} Senshock only enters the fray when victory is assured, striking down weakened foes to solidify his dominance while ensuring his own survival.

{@b Contingency Plans:} Should the Temple face defeat, he executes a precise retreat—first withdrawing to the throne room to deliver his final report to Zuggtmoy before vanishing via teleportation to a hidden sanctuary, leaving the wreckage behind.

entries.name: Hedrack (Supreme Commander)
entries: Senshock outwardly plays the role of Hedrack's trusted advisor while secretly plotting his downfall. He despises Hedrack's grip on the Temple, viewing him as an obstacle to true power. To weaken his rival, Senshock methodically sows discord among Hedrack's subordinates, exploiting their ambitions to erode his authority. By feeding Hedrack selective counsel, he ensures the Supreme Commander remains overconfident and unaware of the growing cracks in his foundation.

entries.name: Barkinar (Commander of Greater Temple Troops)
entries: Senshock regards Barkinar as a blunt instrument—useful but expendable. He keeps the warlord preoccupied with external threats by feeding him false intelligence, ensuring his focus never turns inward. While he spies on Barkinar to gauge his ambitions, he also considers the warrior-priest a potential pawn in a future coup against Hedrack.

entries.name: Deggum (Security Commander)
entries: Senshock sees Deggum's rising influence as a threat and works to keep him firmly under his thumb. He feeds the half-elf a steady stream of minor magical trinkets, fostering a sense of dependency while subtly undermining his confidence. By inundating Deggum with false reports and fabricated crises, Senshock ensures he remains too preoccupied to involve himself in the Temple's greater intrigues. If needed, Deggum may be useful in a coup against Hedrack.

entries.name: Lucius Graeme (Spy Master and Courier)
entries: Lucius Graeme is Senshock's primary informant, though the wizard suspects his loyalties are divided. To control him, Senshock carefully crafts the information he provides, ensuring Lucius remains a valuable but unwitting pawn in his greater game against Hedrack.

entries.name: Falrinth (Rival Wizard and Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death)
entries: Senshock considers Falrinth an arrogant fool, yet a dangerous one. Their rivalry is constant, with both seeking to outmaneuver the other within the Temple's arcane hierarchy. Senshock undermines Falrinth at every turn—dispatching agents to sabotage his magical experiments and spreading insidious rumors to erode his credibility among the other cult leaders.

entries.name: The Four Elemental Priests (Romag, Alrrem, Belsornig, Kelno)
entries: To Senshock, the elemental priests are useful fanatics—too blinded by their faith to see how easily they are manipulated. He maintains a delicate balance, ensuring no one faction gains dominance. By strategically granting magical aid and stoking their rivalries, he keeps them in constant conflict, rendering them too divided to pose a true threat to his own power.

entries.name: Barkinar, Commander of the Greater Temple's Troops
entries: Commander Barkinar is a master of deception and military strategy, overseeing the Greater Temple's armed forces with ruthless efficiency. Once a prominent leader of the Earth Temple, he now serves as one of Hedrack's most trusted subordinates. However, Barkinar harbors a dangerous secret—he has forsaken the Elder Elemental Eye, swearing allegiance to Iuz after a clandestine initiation into the Mysteries of the Greater Temple.

With calculated patience and unwavering ambition, Barkinar has earned Iuz's favor. Should Hedrack fall, he is the most likely successor, though whether he serves Iuz or his own ambitions remains uncertain.

entries.name: Background
entries: Barkinar's path to power began in the service of Hextor, where his hunger for dominance and wealth set him apart. Drawn by the promise of ancient secrets, he arrived at the ruins of the Temple of Elemental Evil with a band of mercenaries after the First Temple's fall. Amid the cult's internal power struggles, Barkinar proved his cunning—betraying his allies and seizing control of the Earth Temple by eliminating its former leader, Landers.

Recognizing his ambition, Hedrack personally initiated Barkinar into Iuz's secret service two years ago. Embracing his new role, Barkinar abandoned the Elder Elemental Eye and pledged himself to the Old One. Yet, ever the strategist, he maintains the illusion of loyalty to the Eye, ensuring that the lesser factions remain unaware of his true allegiance.
{@i "Hedrack is a great man, but greatness fades. When it does, I will be there to carry the banner forward."}

Appearance and Personality: Barkinar is a hardened Oeridian warrior, his presence exuding discipline and quiet menace. He wears battle-scarred, blackened plate armor emblazoned with the flaming eye of the Elder Elemental God, a symbol of both faith and authority. His short-cropped brown hair and meticulously groomed mustache contrast with the ever-present shadow of stubble from long campaigns. Every movement is measured, every word deliberate—a man who commands without raising his voice.

items: {@b Master of Manipulation:} Barkinar walks the razor's edge between loyalty and ambition, serving Iuz while maintaining the illusion of faith in the Elder Elemental Eye
{@b Strategic Enforcer:} Wields discipline and reward with precision, keeping his troops obedient while ensuring their absolute dependence on him.

data.name: Alrrem, Prefect of the Air Temple

entries.name: Deggum, Commander of the Temple Guards
entries: Deggum, a half-elf with a brooding presence, is a rising force within the Greater Temple of Elemental Evil. As commander of the temple guards and their monstrous allies, he wields considerable influence over the cult's security and military actions. His rapid rise stems from a mix of ruthless pragmatism, unshakable loyalty to Hedrack, and an uncanny ability to secure alliances with the Temple's less savory forces.

entries.name: Appearance
entries: Deggum's gaunt frame, lank black hair, and piercing green eyes lend him an eerie, almost spectral quality. His face, perpetually shadowed with exhaustion, bears deep lines of stress and calculation. A pronounced limp does little to slow his predatory movements, making him an unsettling figure even among the Temple's hardened warriors. He wears dusky-hued studded leather armor, enchanted for both protection and silence, and carries a morningstar, crossbow, hunting knife—selected for efficiency in eliminating threats.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Deggum is a ruthless strategist who thrives in the shadows, pulling strings with quiet precision rather than overt displays of power. His unwavering loyalty to Hedrack is born from both admiration and calculation—he sees the Supreme Commander as a model of control and dominance. Unlike his more flamboyant peers, Deggum embodies cold efficiency, enforcing discipline with a careful blend of fear and necessity.

items: {@b Cold-Blooded Tactician:} Anticipates every variable, ensuring no move is made without careful calculation.
{@b Enforcer of Order:} Keeps his troops obedient through a precise balance of intimidation and calculated rewards.
{@b Manipulative Strategist:} Masters the art of deception.

entries.name: Role in the Temple of Elemental Evil
entries: Deggum oversees the Temple's security with a meticulous and ruthless approach, ensuring its defenses remain impenetrable. He coordinates patrols, fortifications, and rapid response units to neutralize intrusions. When threats arise, Deggum personally leads scouting missions, eliminating dangers before they reach the Temple's doorstep. Ever watchful, he remains the last line of defense against those who seek to dismantle the cult.

entries.name: Background
entries: Born in the shadowed groves of Welkwood, Deggum turned his back on his homeland in pursuit of forbidden power. Lareth the Beautiful, a master manipulator, saw potential in the young half-elf and whispered promises of dominance and destiny. Under Lareth's tutelage, Deggum honed his skills in deception, strategy, and coercion. His true ascent began when he brokered an alliance with the Blackthorn ogres under Chief Garghuk, proving his value to Hedrack and securing his place within the Greater Temple's inner circle.

entries.name: Key Events in His Rise to Power

items: {@b Forsaken Heritage:} Cast aside his ties to the elves of Welkwood, choosing power over tradition.
{@b Recruitment by Lareth:} Learned the tenets of the Elder Elemental God.
{@b Ogre Pact:} Secured the allegiance of the Blackthorn ogres, delivering Chief Garghuk's warriors to the Greater Temple.

entries: "I didn't leave Welkwood to cower in the shadows. I came to rise above those who scorned me, and I will." - Deggum

entries.name: Motivations
entries: Deggum is consumed by ambition, seeking to carve out a legacy within the Temple's hierarchy. Though fiercely loyal to Hedrack, he secretly harbors dreams of surpassing him. To solidify his power, Deggum focuses on strengthening his grip over the temple guards and ogre forces, ensuring their unwavering allegiance to him alone.

Despite his public devotion to the Elder Elemental God, Deggum knows the truth—Zuggtmoy, not the Elder Elemental Eye, holds the Temple's reins. He carefully balances his allegiance, outwardly preaching elemental destruction while privately safeguarding his own future within the cult's shifting tides of power.

entries.name: Role in the Temple of Elemental Evil
entries: Deggum commands the Temple's security with ruthless efficiency, ensuring its defenses remain impenetrable. He oversees patrols, fortifications, and rapid-response forces to neutralize intrusions before they escalate. Beyond mere defense, he leads scouting missions to monitor threats and identify potential weaknesses in enemy forces. His influence extends to the Temple's monstrous allies, whom he recruits and indoctrinates, bolstering the cult's military strength under the guise of the Elder Elemental God's prophesied awakening.

items: {@b Fortress Guardian:} Orchestrates defenses, ensuring the Temple's inner sanctum remains unbreachable.
{@b Tactical Expansion:} Strengthens Temple forces by recruiting monstrous allies and mercenaries.
{@b Espionage and Scouting:} Oversees reconnaissance operations, tracking enemy movements and preempting attacks.

entries.name: Political Relationships
entries: As commander, Deggum navigates the temple's volatile political landscape, balancing loyalty to Hedrack while countering rivals within the Greater Temple. Both Hedrack and Senshock personally mentor him, recognizing his potential for leadership. Ever diligent, Deggum carries his spellbook at all times, refining his mastery of divine and arcane magic—many of his spells mirroring Senshock's, ensuring his versatility in both faith and sorcery.

items: {@b Hedrack, Supreme Commander:} One of Hedrack's most trusted subordinates, Deggum receives direct guidance from him.
{@b Senshock, High Wizard:} Deggum respects Senshock's magical prowess but wary of ambitions.
{@b Romag, Earth Temple Leader:} Maintains a tenuous alliance by supplying Romag with ogre forces.

entries: "Let Romag and Belsornig squabble over their petty rivalries. In the end, the one who controls the gates controls the Temple." - Commander Deggum

items: {@b Belsornig, Water Temple Leader:} The two compete for control over the temple's defensive resources.
{@b Commander Feldrin:} Their differing approaches to leadership often place them at odds.

entries.name: Recruitment of the Blackthorn Tribe
entries: Deggum's most valuable contribution to the temple is securing the allegiance of the Blackthorn Tribe, a brutal force of ogres, bugbears, and hobgoblins. Through the influence of Iuz and careful negotiation, he brokered a pact with Chieftain Garghuk the Bloodtooth, providing the temple with a steady stream of warriors—driven by fear rather than loyalty.

items: {@b Negotiation with Garghuk:} Maintaining a delicate balance of fear / reward to keep the chieftain in line.
{@b Deployment of Forces:} Strategically placing ogres within key positions across the temple's various levels, including the Greater Temple.
{@b Oversight of Ogre Leadership:} Managing Chieftain Gloarok in the Greater Temple and Gerard, who assists Romag in the Earth Temple.
{@b Intelligence Gathering:} Utilizing Blackthorn scouts to gather intelligence on rival factions, adventurers, and local threats around Nulb and Hommlet.

entries: "Hedrack sees potential in me, and I will not disappoint. The ogres follow because they fear me… as they should. Loyalty bought with fear and blood is the only kind that matters."

entries.name: Feldrin, of the Temple's Guard
entries: A cunning strategist and ruthless opportunist, Feldrin, a half-Oeridian, half-Flan rogue, is a master of deception, smuggling, and underhanded dealings. As commander of the Temple's brigands, bandits, and enforcers, he maintains control not through brute strength, but through guile, blackmail, and well-placed alliances. With deep criminal connections and a keen understanding of leverage, Feldrin ensures the smooth flow of stolen goods, captives, and supplies to the Greater Temple while keeping the more violent elements of his forces in line. Though he commands warriors, he is no warlord—he is a thief first and foremost, using cunning over combat, and ensuring his position remains secure through manipulation rather than direct confrontation.

entries.name: Role and Importance
entries: Feldrin commands the Temple's guards and bandit forces, responsible for both defense and supply logistics.

items: {@b Political Operative:} Trusted by Supreme Commander Hedrack, Feldrin also serves as an emissary to King Korvash Bonegnaw, ruler of the gnolls of the Jagged Fangs tribe in the Lortmils.
{@b Supply Chain:} Manages the transport of resources to the Greater Temple's operations.

entries: "Control isn't about loyalty—it's about leverage. Keep everyone hungry enough to need you, but fed enough not to bite."

entries.name: Feldrin's Operations
entries: Feldrin resides in Area 243a on the Temple's second dungeon level, where his lavish quarters reflect his wealth and importance. His operation relies on a structured system for securing supplies and prisoners.

items: {@b Initial Capture:} Bandits, led by Gattas Void, raid settlements near the Moathouse.
{@b Sorting & Distribution:} Loot is processed at Gefreid Patris' Hunting Cottage near Nulb.
{@b Final Transport:} Goods and captives move through a hidden tunnel into the Greater Temple.

{@b Hedrack's Trust:} Feldrin holds a degree of favor with Supreme Commander Hedrack, who relies on him for sensitive missions such as negotiating with King Korvash Bonegnaw. However, this trust is conditional—Hedrack values results, but Feldrin's self-serving nature and questionable loyalty make him a potential liability.

{@b Brunk's Ambitions:} Feldrin's half-orc lieutenant, Brunk, harbors a deep resentment toward his superior and openly covets his position. He is constantly looking for opportunities to undermine or eliminate Feldrin, provided he can do so without exposing his own treachery.
{@b Spying Missions:} Feldrin and Brunk often use disguises to spy in the local villages.

{@b Bandit Allies:} Feldrin maintains alliances with Smigmal Redhand and her bandit crew, though his self-serving nature makes his loyalty tenuous. While he recognizes their value, he sees them as tools rather than true allies.

entries: "Adventurers think themselves heroes. They're just another kind of bandit—only louder and more self-righteous." - Feldrin

data.name: Belsornig, Canon of the Water Temple

entries.name: Falrinth, Master Wizard, Keeper of the Orb of Golden Death
entries: A cunning and duplicitous figure, Falrinth operates within the third level of the Temple of Elemental Evil. Though he holds no formal allegiance to the elemental temples or the Greater Temple, his scheming nature makes him a key player in the Temple's politics and intrigue. An opportunist driven by ambition, he carefully balances alliances and rivalries, ensuring his own survival while keeping his true motives concealed.

entries.name: Rogue Wizard
entries: While not an official member of the Greater Temple, Falrinth is deeply embedded in its web of deception. As an 8th-level magic-user, he operates independently, both a valuable ally and a potential threat to the leadership.
"They say the Elder Elemental God whispers in the dark. I've heard those whispers… and one day, I will whisper back."

Falrinth's connection to his quasit familiar, Kriitch, allows him to cast as a 9th-level wizard when within a one-mile radius. Though he outwardly serves Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders, his true allegiance is to power itself. He schemes to advance his own goals, betraying even Lolth if it means seizing greater control.

items: {@b Ambition:} Seeks power above all else, even if it means betraying Lolth.
{@b Duplicitous Nature:} His unpredictability makes him a potential ally or deadly foe.

entries.name: Location and Role
entries: Falrinth resides in Area 337 on the third level of the Temple, where he safeguards his secrets and resources. His chambers connect to an Escape Tunnel, a hidden route leading to the Hunting Cottage, used by bandits allied with Smigmal Redhand.
"The Nodes are not prisons; they are gateways. Those who master them will rule both this world and the next." - Falrinth

entries.name: The Orb of Golden Death
entries: Falrinth secretly possesses the Orb of Golden Death, an artifact tied to the Elder Elemental God and central to the Temple's power. He keeps the Orb hidden from his allies and superiors, viewing it as his ultimate bargaining chip or tool for personal ascension.

items: {@b Connection to the Nodes:} The Orb serves as a key to unlocking and controlling the elemental nodes.
{@b Secrecy:} Falrinth's concealment of the Orb makes him a target for both the Greater Temple and adventurers.

entries.name: Legendary Equipment
entries: {@item Orb of Golden Death}: Allows scrying and enhances necromantic spells.
{@item Mirror of Mental Prowess}: Can detect thoughts and manipulate reflections.
{@item Tome of Leadership}: Grants Charisma bonus if studied.
{@item Vacuous Grimoire}: A cursed book that drains Intelligence.

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@i Dagger. Melee Weapon Attack: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 4 (1d4+3) piercing damage.}

entries.name: Role and Loyalties
entries: Falrinth maintains a loose alliance with Smigmal Redhand and her bandit crew, including Commander Feldrin, who operates from the Broken Tower. However, his true loyalty lies only with himself. Though he cooperates with these factions, Falrinth is ultimately an opportunist, always seeking the greatest personal advantage.

He also collaborates with Lareth the Beautiful, both serving as agents of Lolth, the Demon Queen of Spiders. Their shared mission, as dictated by Lolth, is to gain control of the elemental nodes (Nexus), though the full scope of their plan remains hidden.

entries.name: Senshock: A Cautious Superior
entries: Falrinth reports to Senshock, High Commander of the Greater Temple, but their relationship is based on mutual distrust. Senshock sees Falrinth as a useful but untrustworthy resource, aware that his loyalty to Lolth and secrecy regarding the Orb of Golden Death could threaten the Greater Temple's unity.

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand & Feldrin: Bandit Connections
entries: {@b Smigmal Redhand:} An ally of convenience, Falrinth works with her bandits, though his loyalty is tenuous at best.
{@b Commander Feldrin:} While Feldrin reports to Falrinth indirectly through Smigmal, this chain of loyalty is weak and unreliable.

entries.name: Lareth the Beautiful: A Dangerous Partnership
entries: {@b Lolth's Plan:} Both serve Lolth, working to manipulate the elemental nodes, possibly undermining Zuggtmoy and the Elder Elemental God.
{@b Fragile Alliance:} Despite their shared devotion to Lolth, ambition makes their alliance precarious and could lead to betrayal.

entries.name: Falrinth's Tactics and Abilities
entries: A cautious strategist, Falrinth relies on magic, deception, and his quasit familiar to outmaneuver threats.
"Hedrack and his ilk think themselves untouchable, yet they dance like puppets on strings they cannot see. Strings I can cut."

items: {@b Kriitch the Quasit:} Spies in giant centipede form, relaying information.
{@b Escape Tunnel:} Enables quick retreats or ambushes.
{@b Preparedness:} Uses divination and illusions to manipulate encounters.

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand, Commander
entries: A ruthless and cunning half-orc Fighter/Assassin, Smigmal Redhand commands the outer defenses of the Temple of Elemental Evil. Leading mercenaries and bandits from the ruined tower, she serves as the Temple's first line of defense, eliminating threats with cold efficiency. Using a Hat of Disguise, she masquerades as an elf, blending deception with assassination to manipulate both high society and adventurers who stray too close.

entries.name: Master of Deception and Deadly Precision
entries: Smigmal is an imposing six-foot figure, her lean, sinewy frame hardened by years of battle and subterfuge. Her sharp, angular features and piercing yellow eyes betray her orcish blood, though few ever see her true face. Under her magical elven guise, she appears as a refined noblewoman, effortlessly infiltrating enemy ranks and social circles.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Smigmal is a cold, calculating assassin with an iron will. She believes in striking from the shadows and maintaining absolute control over her surroundings. Her loyalty is primarily to Falrinth, her lover and secret ally within the temple, but she harbors ambitions of her own. Ruthless, pragmatic, and untrusting, Smigmal excels at manipulating others to serve her interests.

items: {@b Calculating and Ruthless:} Smigmal values efficiency over morality, always seeking the most pragmatic solution.
{@b Devoted to Falrinth:} She prioritizes her relationship with the wizard over temple politics.
{@b Paranoia and Mistrust:} Never truly trusts her superiors, always keeping an escape route in mind.

entries: "Hedrack plays the king, Senshock schemes for the throne, and Barkinar waits like a vulture. I simply watch and sharpen my knives." - Smigmal

entries.name: Smigmal's Background
entries: Smigmal hails from the war-torn Bandit Kingdoms, where survival meant mastering cunning and violence. Her lethal skills caught the attention of Temple agents, leading to her swift rise through the ranks by eliminating threats and proving herself to Supreme Commander Hedrack and Senshock.

items: {@b Mercenary Roots:} Raised among cutthroats and raiders, she learned to fight, deceive, and survive.
{@b Temple Recruitment:} Started as a scout, later placed in command of the outer defenses.
{@b Elven Disguise:} Uses a magical hat to pose as an elven diplomat, gathering intelligence in Nulb and Hommlet.

entries.name: Role in the Temple
entries: As leader of the Temple's surface defenses, Smigmal commands raiding parties and ensures no enemy infiltrates the ruined tower stronghold.

items: {@b Lead Defensive Operations:} Orchestrates raids and ambushes against Temple enemies.
{@b Espionage and Sabotage:} Works with Falrinth to spy on enemies and rivals.
{@b Mercenary Control:} Manages Temple-hired forces, ensuring loyalty through fear.

entries: "{@i Obedience comes not from loyalty, but from fear. And fear is a currency I deal in generously.}" - Smigmal

entries.name: Falrinth (Lover and Wizard Ally)
entries: Smigmal's closest ally and lover, Falrinth, uses her position to gather intelligence and eliminate rivals. His quasit familiar provides her with a steady flow of spying reports from Nulb, the Temple, and beyond.

However, Falrinth's ambitions outweigh his loyalty. He sees Smigmal as a pawn in his quest for power, hoping to seize control after Hedrack's fall. Smigmal, aware of his grandiose dreams, knows he will abandon the Temple if it collapses—and she plans accordingly.

entries.name: Hedrack (Supreme Commander)
entries: Hedrack tolerates Smigmal due to her effectiveness, but distrusts her closeness to Falrinth, whose loyalty to Lolth makes him a wildcard. He keeps a watchful eye on her, using her to maintain order but never fully trusting her.

entries.name: Deggum (Commander of Security)
entries: As her direct superior, Deggum relies on Smigmal for defense reports and coordination. Though she respects his tactical mind, she sees him as a roadblock to her ambitions and remains wary of his authority.
"{@i Barkinar may wear the armor, but it's his boots that tell the truth—always pointed toward the exit. He's waiting, watching, but when the time comes, he'll run.}" - Smigmal

entries.name: Devotion to the Elder Elemental God
entries: Smigmal, like many in the temple, pays lip service to the Elder Elemental God but does not hold true faith. She sees the cult's chaos as a useful distraction for her own agendas but has no desire to awaken the ancient power.
"{@i Hedrack plays the king, Senshock schemes for the throne, and Barkinar waits like a vulture. I simply watch and sharpen my knives.}" - Smigmal

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand
entries: {@i Medium Humanoid (Half-Orc), Neutral Evil}
{@i Armor Class 19 (+1 Leather Armor, +2 Shield)}
{@i Hit Points 52 (7d8+21)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Savings Throws Dex +6, Int +5}
{@i Skills Acrobatics +9, Deception +5, Intimidation +5, Stealth +9, Sleight of Hand +6}
{@i Senses passive Perception 8}
{@i Languages Common, Orc}
{@i Challenge 5 (1,800 XP)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Assassinate.} During her first turn, Smigmal has advantage on attack rolls against any creature that hasn't taken a turn. Any hit she scores against a surprised creature is a critical hit.
{@b Evasion.} When Smigmal is subjected to an effect that allows her to make a Dexterity saving throw for half damage, she instead takes no damage on a success and half damage on a failure.
{@b Relentless Endurance (1/Day).} When Smigmal is reduced to 0 hit points, instead drops to 1 hp, unless she is killed outright.
{@b Savage Attacks.} When Smigmal scores a critical hit with a melee weapon attack, she can roll one additional weapon damage die and add it to the extra damage.

{@b Sneak Attack (1/Turn).} Smigmal deals an extra 14 (4d6) damage when she hits a target with a weapon attack and has advantage on the attack roll, or when the target is within 5 feet of an ally and she doesn't have disadvantage on the attack roll.

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Multiattack.} Smigmal makes two melee attacks.
{@b +1 Shortsword.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 7 (1d6+4) piercing damage.
{@b Hand Crossbow.} {@i Ranged Weapon Attack:} +6 to hit, range 30/120 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 6 (1d6+3) piercing damage.

{@b Poisoned Blade (Recharge 5-6).} Smigmal coats her blade with an insinuative poison (Type B). The next creature she hits must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw, taking 25 poison damage over 2 rounds on a failure, or half on a success.

entries.name: Equipment
entries: +1 Leather Armor, +2 Shield, +1 Shortsword, Hand Crossbow with 10 Bolts, {@item Ring of Invisibility}, Jeweled Belt (3,200 gp), Earrings (400 gp each), Secret Compartment (30 Bloodstones, 2 Pieces of Jewelry, 50 pp in a hidden belt pocket)

entries.name: Lucius Graeme, Temple Agent (Courier)
entries: Lucius Graeme, a Oeridian/Suel operative, commands attention—not through stature, but through an aura of quiet menace. His portly frame, draped in simple gray garments, conceals a calculating and dangerous mind. A small gray hat often shadows his scarred face, marked by an old injury from falling debris. His golden, asymmetrical eyes seem to uncover secrets no one wishes to reveal, and his smooth, unsettling voice has a way of disarming or unnerving listeners.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Once a vain and cruel nobleman, Lucius's fall from grace left him with a bitter hatred for order and morality. Now, he is a manipulative schemer, wielding intelligence and deception like a blade. Beneath his calm exterior simmers a barely controlled rage, which he unleashes without mercy when provoked.

items: {@b Master Manipulator:} Feigns weakness, allowing enemies to underestimate him.
{@b Chaos as Power:} Believes disorder breeds opportunity, shaping the world in his image.
{@b Devotion to Elemental Forces:} Sees the primordial powers of the elemental planes as neither good nor evil—only tools for the strong.

entries: "{@i Chaos is the truest form of control—those who embrace it will shape the world to their will.}" - Aldren

entries.name: The Art of Deception
entries: Lucius delights in playing the fool, allowing others to underestimate him while he quietly gathers intelligence and consolidates power. He thrives in chaos, seeing it as the ultimate tool of control. To him, the elemental planes are not moral forces, but raw engines of destruction and renewal, waiting to be harnessed by those strong enough to command them.
"{@i Civilization is a fragile mask over the face of the elemental chaos. It's a shame when it breaks… or a delight.}" - Lucius Graeme

entries.name: Lucius Graeme
entries: {@i Human Cleric Level 7, Neutral Evil}
{@i Armor Class 16 (chain shirt, shield)}
{@i Hit Points 45 (7d8-7)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Savings Throws Wisdom +6, Charisma +5}
{@i Skills Deception +5, Insight +6, Persuasion +5, Religion +5}
{@i Senses passive Perception 13}
{@i Languages Common, Abyssal, Gnome}
{@i Challenge 5 (1800 exp)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Dark Devotion (Cleric of Evil).} Lucius has advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.
{@b Fanatical Conviction.} When Lucius is reduced to 0 hit points, he can make a DC 10 Constitution saving throw. On a success, he drops to 1 hit point instead.

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: Spells Prepared (Spell Save DC 13, +5 to hit with spell attacks)
{@b Cantrips (at will):} {@spell Thaumaturgy}, {@spell Sacred Flame}, {@spell Spare the Dying}
{@b 1st level (4 slots):} {@spell Command} (x3), {@spell Cause Light Wounds}, {@spell Detect Magic}, {@spell Protection from Good}
{@b 2nd level (3 slots):} {@spell Detect Charm} (x2), {@spell Find Traps}, {@spell Hold Person} (x2), {@spell Silence} (15 ft. radius)
{@b 3rd level (2 slots):} {@spell Animate Dead}, {@spell Continual Darkness}, {@spell Dispel Magic}, {@spell Bestow Curse}
{@b 4th level (1 slot):} {@spell Control Water} (Elemental-themed control over the Elder Elemental God's power).
{@b Divination:} Used to communicate with the EEG (Zyggtmoy).

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Mace of the Withering.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +7 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 8 (1d6+4) bludgeoning damage. The target must make a DC 13 Constitution saving throw or suffer 1 level of exhaustion. If the target is frightened, the attack deals an additional 1d6 necrotic damage.
{@b Unholy Smite (1/Day).} When Lucius hits with a melee attack, he deals an additional 14 (4d6) necrotic damage.

{@b Cloak of the Forsaken Veil (1/Day).} Lucius can cast {@spell Pass Without Trace} on himself without expending a spell slot. He also has advantage on Dexterity (Stealth) checks while wearing cloak.

{@b Amulet of the Shrouded One.} Lucius's alignment and location cannot be detected by divination magic (Detect Evil and Good, Commune, Divination, Scrying, etc.). He cannot be tracked by nonmagical means unless he allows it. Once per long rest, can cast {@spell Nondetection} on himself without expending a spell slot.

entries.name: Political Relationships and Influence
entries: Lucius Graeme is the Temple of Elemental Evil's most trusted courier and facilitator, operating in secrecy to connect its agents across Verbobonc, Nulb, and Hommlet. While not a direct leader, his role is crucial, ensuring covert communication between Hedrack and the Temple's external networks, all while manipulating powerful individuals behind the scenes.

entries.name: Lakash Quallad (Brother of Lady Nysera)
entries: A wealthy merchant in Verbobonc City, Lakash moves stolen goods, secures bribes, and launders Temple funds. Lucius ensures his loyalty, using him to manage financial transactions and smuggling routes.

entries.name: Lord Conner Bridgeforth (Political Manipulator)
entries: A noble with political influence, Bridgeforth steers court rumors and noble alliances to serve the Temple's agenda. Lucius acts as his handler, ensuring his actions benefit the cult while keeping him from arousing suspicion.

entries.name: Madame Selentis (Nysera Krivaltis)
entries: Formerly Lady Nysera Krivaltis, occupies a crucial yet covert role within the Temple of Elemental Evil's political and logistical framework in Nulb. Stripped of her title and presumed dead following her family's disgrace after the Battle of Emridy Meadows in 569 CY, she now operates under an alias as Hedrack's chief overseer in Nulb, managing critical supply lines, smuggling routes, and personnel transfers. Though officially distanced from her past identity, her ambition remains potent, now redirected fully into serving the Temple's sinister aims.

entries.name: Agents in Hommlet
entries: Lucius Graeme oversees a network of spies and saboteurs in Hommlet, ensuring Temple influence remains hidden while disrupting local defenses.

items: {@b Gremag Gozlin (D&G Mercantile):} A shopkeeper-spy who tracks adventurer movements and relays reports to Lucius via coded messages.
{@b Joseph Millar (Doomwatch Construction Site):} A saboteur delaying the keep's construction, weakening Verbobonc's defensive efforts.
{@b Zert (Inn of the Welcome Wench):} A deadly agent posing as a mercenary, tasked with monitoring and eliminating threats.

entries.name: Agents in Verbobonc City
entries: Lucius's influence in Verbobonc City is stealthy but far-reaching, ensuring Temple gold flows through illicit trade routes while using noble connections to manipulate politics.

items: {@b Lakash Quallad's Merchant Network:} Launders stolen goods and bribes officials.
{@b Lord Bridgeforth:} Uses his noble status to steer political intrigue and suppress investigations.

entries.name: Operations in Nulb
entries: Lucius Graeme serves as the Temple of Elemental Evil's hidden hand in Nulb, ensuring the village remains a safe haven for cultists, mercenaries, and smugglers. His delicate alliance with Madame Selentis allows him to facilitate supplies, coin, and influence without drawing attention. Operating under the guise of a harmless gambler at the Waterside Hostel, he manipulates events from the shadows.

items: {@b Mutual Benefit:} Lucius supports Selentis's bid for power in Nulb, viewing her as a valuable intermediary between the Temple and local factions. In return, she secures trade routes and ensures that bandit and pirate resources support Temple efforts.
{@b Information Exchange:} Lucius feeds Selentis intelligence on rival factions such as Dick Rentsch and Captain Garik Marath, while she funnels key information back to the Greater Temple.

{@b Selentis's Independence:} While willing to collaborate, Selentis's true ambition is reclaiming her family's honor, which doesn't always align with the Temple's agenda. Lucius keeps her under close watch, wary of potential betrayal.

{@b Conflict of Interests:} Lucius remains loyal to the Greater Temple, prioritizing domination and control, while Selentis seeks local influence. When necessary, he undermines her to prevent her from becoming a liability.

entries.name: The Gambler's Mask – Cover Identity
entries: Lucius rarely acts openly, preferring to gather intelligence and manipulate events while maintaining a low profile.

items: {@b False Persona:} He masquerades as a harmless drunkard and gambler, lurking at the Waterside Hostel, always listening.
{@b Extracting Intel:} Through casual conversation and strategic questioning, he collects vital intelligence on new arrivals, merchant routes, and potential threats.
{@b Telltale Signs:} Observant adventurers might notice inconsistencies in his stories, his keen interest in security, or the way his gaze lingers on important discussions.

entries: "{@i Nature, civilization, good, evil—they're all pawns to the primordial forces that truly rule this world.}" - Alden

entries.name: Meeting Aldren (Lucius)
entries: Aldren, draped in a well-worn cloak and clutching a weathered merchant's satchel, approaches the adventurers with a practiced, disarming smile. His smooth, slightly unnerving voice carries a friendly yet probing tone as he speaks: "Ah, travelers! A fine day to find company on these lonely roads, wouldn't you agree? Allow me to introduce myself—just a humble merchant peddling wares and tales alike. The name's Aldren. Been wandering these hills for years, trading with the gnomes and passing adventurers like yourselves. Always an adventure out here, isn't it?"
He adjusts his satchel, letting the faint glimmer of trinkets and oddities peek out, an inviting distraction as his golden eyes linger on each party member, assessing them carefully.

"Tell me, friends, what brings you to the Kron Hills? It's not often I see a group like yours—armed and armored, yet with the air of purpose about you. Are you hunting treasure, seeking glory, or perhaps delivering some noble cause? The hills have no shortage of mysteries, I assure you."
Aldren leans forward conspiratorially, his tone dropping slightly, as if sharing a secret.

"These parts have their dangers, mind you. Not just the usual bandits or wild beasts. Strange things—whispers of old powers stirring, unnatural storms, and… well, I hear tales of the Greenway Valley being less safe for travelers these days. Makes a man curious about what might draw someone like you to these parts. And perhaps… how I might be of assistance?"
His voice softens further, laced with subtle charm and intrigue.
"After all, we're all travelers here. A bit of information for a bit of coin—or a friendly exchange of stories. Who knows? Maybe our paths cross for a reason, eh?"
Aldren smiles warmly, his golden eyes glinting faintly with a mix of feigned innocence and calculated intent.

entries.name: Final Confrontation
entries: As the adventurers dismantle the elemental temples and cripple the cult's operations, Lucius Graeme grows desperate. In a final gambit, he unleashes the power of the Devastation Orb, aiming to turn the tide of battle through catastrophic elemental destruction. This confrontation will be a high-stakes test of the players' strategy and resilience.

items: {@b Divide & Conquer:} Lucius uses deception and misdirection to separate the adventurers, creating opportunities for ambushes.
{@b Psychological Warfare:} He manipulates the players, exploiting their personal motivations and sowing doubt among allies.
{@b Strategic Retreats:} If overwhelmed, he falls back to prearranged escape routes, using illusions, traps, and summoned creatures to delay pursuit.

entries: {@b Princes of the Apocolypse} - Reference the Devastation Orb used by elemental cultists. Wizards of the Coast LLC

entries.name: Personality in Combat Tactics
entries: A master manipulator, Lucius avoids direct confrontation, instead wielding deception, bribery, and assassination to further the Temple's influence. He rarely acts alone, deploying a vast network of agents and informants to carry out his schemes while keeping his own hands clean.

entries.name: Strategic Actions
entries: In battle, Lucius is a ruthless strategist, using psychological warfare to taunt and manipulate his foes. He mocks their failures, aiming to unnerve and divide them, exploiting their motivations and doubts. However, his overconfidence may be his undoing—Lucius underestimates the adventurers' resilience and adaptability, believing himself always in control.

items: Maintains Temple communication across Verbobonc, Nulb, and Hommlet.
Bribes and blackmails nobles and merchants to align them with Temple interests.
Oversees smuggling and caravan raids, ensuring stolen goods support the cult's expansion.
Eliminates threats quietly, using assassins to remove problematic individuals without exposing himself.

entries.name: Temple Operative
entries: A priest of Iuz and Temple operative, Lucius Graeme was sent to serve Supreme Commander Hedrack. A master of ambition and chaos, he soon adapted, becoming a courier and facilitator for the Greater Temple, overseeing communication between Hommlet, Nulb, and the elemental outposts.
"{@i I do not kill when I can persuade, nor destroy when I can corrupt. Why break when one can bend?}"

entries.name: Romag, High Priest
entries: Romag, the self-proclaimed Prophet of Earth, leads the Cult of the Black Earth within the Temple of Elemental Evil. Unlike his more ambitious rivals, Romag rose to power by inheriting the foundation laid by his predecessor, Barkinar. A pragmatic leader and skilled manipulator, he focuses on defense and fortification rather than expansion, though his position is constantly challenged by internal dissent and external rivalries.

entries.name: Prophet of Earth
entries: Romag is an imposing figure of Oeridian/Flan descent, hardened by years of surviving the Temple's brutal hierarchy. His earthen-toned robes, adorned with gemstones infused with elemental power, reflect his devotion to the Black Earth.

items: Stout, weathered physique, muscular from years of physical endurance.
Brown and gray robes, symbolizing the resilience of the earth.
Wields a stone-etched warhammer, imbued with elemental magic.

entries.name: Personality & Leadership
entries: Romag is pragmatic, cautious, and calculating, but he lacks the charisma and ambition of other high priests. He prioritizes stability over conquest, fortifying his temple's defenses to withstand both internal power struggles and external threats.
However, his rule is fragile—unlike his predecessors, he struggles to inspire absolute fear or devotion, making him a constant target for ambitious subordinates.

items: {@b Defensive Focus:} Strengthens temple defenses rather than expanding influence.
{@b Calculating but Cautious:} Uses manipulation and alliances to maintain power.
{@b Struggles with Control:} Lacks the fearsome authority to suppress all dissent.

entries.name: Earth Temple's Role in the Cult of the Elder Elemental God
entries: Unlike the Greater Temple leaders, Romag and his followers are true believers in the Elder Elemental God. They see themselves as the chosen harbingers of the god's return and work tirelessly to fulfill its will. Earth Temple clerics wield powers related to stone manipulation, fortification, and crushing their foes.

entries.name: Romag's Forces
entries: Romag commands a disciplined and growing military force, structured under his authoritarian rule. His soldiers and clerics form the Elemental Earth Reavers, a formidable force that aims to crush opposition.

items: Hartsch (Level 2 Cleric, Romag's Adept)
40 Human Temple Guards
20 Gnolls
2 Hobgoblins
Bugbear (enforcer and bodyguard)

entries.name: Romag, High Priest
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Cleric level 4, lawful evil}
{@i Armor Class 16 (chain mail +2)}
{@i Hit Points 28 (4d8+12)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Saving Throws Wis +4, Cha +3}
{@i Skills Religion +3, Persuasion +4}
{@i Senses passive Perception 13}
{@i Languages Common, Terran}
{@i Challenge 1 (200 XP)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Dark Blessing.} Romag has advantage on saving throws against being frightened or charmed.
{@b Earth's Resilience (1/Day).} As a reaction, Romag can gain resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, and slashing damage for 1 minute.

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: Romag is a 4th-level spellcaster (spell save DC 12, +4 to hit with spell attacks). He has the following prepared:
{@b Cantrips (at will):} {@spell Magic Stone}, {@spell Guidance}, {@spell Mold Earth}
{@b 1st level (3 slots):} {@spell Command}, {@spell Cure Wounds}, {@spell Bane}, {@spell Earth Tremor}
{@b 2nd level (2 slots):} {@spell Hold Person}, {@spell Earthbind}, {@spell Resist Fire}

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Mace +1.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 7 (1d6+3) bludgeoning damage.
{@b Earth's Grip (Recharge 5-6).} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} Romag causes the ground in a 10-ft. radius to tremble for 1 minute. Creatures moving through the area treat it as difficult terrain.

entries.name: Origins and Early Life
entries: Romag's rise to power was not one of glory or divine providence, but of betrayal, endurance, and revenge. Born into a minor Oeridian noble house in Verbobonc, he spent his youth struggling under his father's failing landholdings. Dissatisfied with his fate, Romag abandoned his family's burdens and sought power in the priesthood, drawn to the forbidden teachings of the Elder Elemental God, which promised strength through elemental might.
His journey led him to a band of like-minded outcasts:

items: Dick Rentsch, a cunning manipulator.
Dala, Dick's ruthless lover.
Senshock, an ambitious wizard.

entries: Together, they delved into lost ruins, searching for wealth and power. Though Romag served as their spiritual guide, his influence was overshadowed by Senshock's magic and Dick's scheming.
"{@i You cannot bargain with stone. You cannot plead with earth. You will break before I do.}" -Romag

entries.name: Betrayal in the Dwarven Stronghold
entries: The group's most infamous expedition led them into a forgotten dwarven stronghold deep within the Lortmil Mountains. Hoping to unearth wealth and arcane secrets, they ventured too far and encountered the fortress's ancient guardians—dwarven constructs and stone-clad sentinels. As the battle turned against them, Senshock made a ruthless decision—he sacrificed Romag, abandoning him to the enemy while ensuring his own escape alongside Dick and Dala.

Captured by the dwarves, Romag endured years of imprisonment—harsh interrogations, isolation, and the gnawing torment of betrayal. Yet within the stone walls of his prison, he deepened his connection to the power of earth, drawing strength from the very stone around him, biding his time for an opportunity to break free.
