"{@i I was buried once, left to rot beneath stone and soil. Now, I am the mountain that will crush them all.}" -Romag

Meanwhile, Senshock returned to the Temple of Elemental Evil, leveraging their expedition's treasures to secure a high-ranking position within the Greater Temple, while Dick and Dala settled in Nulb, using their ill-gotten wealth to establish the Waterside Hostel, a hub for illicit dealings and information gathering.
"{@i Senshock thinks himself untouchable, but even the tallest towers crumble when their foundations crack.}"

entries.name: Escape and the Path to Revenge
entries: After years of brutal captivity, Romag finally saw his chance for freedom during a skirmish within the dwarven stronghold. Using his deepened connection to earth magic and his understanding of subterranean passages, he orchestrated a daring escape, emerging into the wilderness as a changed man—hardened, vengeful, and more determined than ever.

His sole purpose became revenge—not only against those who betrayed him but against all who doubted his strength. Romag made his way back to civilization, following whispers of his former companions' whereabouts until he arrived at Nulb, where he found Dick Rentsch and Dala running the Waterside Hostel.

entries.name: A Reckoning in Nulb
entries: Fueled by rage and betrayal, Romag confronted Dick and Dala, nearly killing them both in a violent outburst. However, the cunning duo turned his fury toward Senshock, painting him as the true traitor who orchestrated Romag's downfall. They pledged their loyalty to Romag, convincing him that Senshock's power was the true prize and that revenge would be best served from within the Temple itself.

Seeing opportunity in their offer, Romag spared them, forging an uneasy alliance. With Dick and Dala's knowledge of the Temple's politics and Nulb's underworld, Romag carefully reintegrated himself into the cult, using his old ties to Senshock to feign forgiveness and renewed loyalty.

entries.name: Climbing the Ranks
entries: Senshock, sensing an opportunity to control Romag, arranged for him to become High Priest of the Earth Temple after Barkinar's promotion to the Greater Temple. However, despite his new title, Romag found himself leading the weakest of the four elemental factions.

Fire Temple Leader Alrrem viewed him as a cowardly fool, unworthy of his station. Water Temple High Priest Belsornig sought to manipulate him, using his faction as a pawn in his own power plays. Aware of his precarious position, Romag leveraged Dick and Dala's information network in Nulb to spy on his rivals. Slowly, he fed critical intelligence to Senshock and Barkinar, gaining favor within the Greater Temple while secretly hoarding knowledge for his own purposes.

Romag also expanded the Earth Temple's influence, quietly recruiting miners, mercenaries, and enforcers under the pretense of temple fortifications. While others saw the Earth Temple as stagnant, Romag was laying the groundwork for his own power play.

entries.name: The Long Game – A Path to Revenge
entries: Yet, Romag's true goal remained unfulfilled—the downfall of Senshock. Knowing that open confrontation was suicidal, he patiently played the long game, feigning loyalty and submission, waiting for the perfect moment to strike.

entries.name: Political Relationships
entries: Romag sees himself as the stabilizing force within the Temple of Elemental Evil, believing Elemental Earth represents the temple's true foundation of power. He views the other elemental factions as reckless and undisciplined, favoring patience and fortification to slowly undermine them. Though outwardly loyal to Supreme Commander Hedrack and the Greater Temple, Romag harbors ambitions of expanding his influence, though his cautious nature prevents him from making bold moves like his rivals.
"{@i Nulb is a swamp of fools and thieves, but even mud can serve a purpose if you shape it well.}" - Romag

entries.name: Influence in Nulb and Rivalry with Madame Selentis
entries: Romag exercises subtle yet significant influence in Nulb through the manipulative dealings of Dick Rentsch, proprietor of the Waterside Hostel. Dick Rentsch, alongside his associate Dala, provides Romag a constant influx of recruits, spies, and intelligence on Nulb's shifting underworld. Yet, Romag's grip over Nulb faces continuous challenge from Madame Selentis (Lady Nysera), whose position as Hedrack's logistics overseer in Nulb threatens Romag's activities.

Romag resents Madame Selentis's growing influence, viewing her efforts to centralize power and command Nulb's illicit networks as dangerously counterproductive to his own careful plans. Though their rivalry remains largely subtle and cloaked in deniability, Romag constantly seeks ways to subvert her authority without provoking direct retaliation from Hedrack or Lucius.

items: {@b Dick Rentsch and Dala:} Essential informants and recruiters, though Romag trusts neither. Dick and Dala's constant pursuit of self-interest and coin makes them unreliable; Romag constantly seeks leverage over them.
{@b Senshock:} A dangerous rival whom Romag pretends to serve while plotting his downfall.
{@b Barkinar:} Once a mentor, now an obstacle to Romag's ambitions within the Temple.

{@b Madame Selentis (Nysera Krivaltis) (Major Rival):} Romag's primary adversary within Nulb; their ongoing power struggle shapes local Temple politics. He carefully undermines her logistical arrangements where possible, sabotaging minor shipments and planting misinformation to diminish her credibility.

{@b Belsornig, Water Temple:} Constantly attempts to maneuver Romag into his schemes, viewing him as a potentially useful pawn, carefully maintains distance, wary of Belsornig's network and manipulations.
{@b Alrrem, Fire Temple:} Holds contempt for Romag, seeing the Earth Temple as passive and weak.

entries.name: Political Maneuvering - Greater Temple
entries: Romag's measured tactics on the following principles:

items: {@b Patience Over Recklessness:} Romag invests long-term in spies, blackmail, and sabotage.
{@b Denial and Distance:} Maintains plausible deniability for all clandestine activities. Even Lucius Graeme struggles to conclusively trace Romag's influence.
{@b Steady Consolidation of Influence:} Prefers incremental advances, building an unassailable base of power before openly challenging rivals.

entries.name: Supreme Commander Hedrack
entries: {@b Thoughts:} Views Hedrack as indulgent and too distracted by devotion to Iuz, leaving the Temple unstable.
{@b Strategy:} Maintains outward loyalty, subtly undermines Hedrack's efficiency through careful manipulation.

entries.name: Canon Belsornig of the Water Temple
entries: {@b Thoughts:} Considers Belsornig his most cunning and dangerous rival.
{@b Strategy:} Deals cautiously; covertly sabotages Water Temple's supply lines to divert resources.
"{@i Hedrack sits atop this Temple like a fat frog on a lily pad. Sooner or later, the pond will dry up.}" - Belsornig

entries.name: Prefect Alrrem of the Fire Temple
entries: {@b Thoughts:} Sees Alrrem as reckless, threatening long-term stability.
{@b Strategy:} Strengthens defenses, alerts Hedrack to Alrrem's instability, uses gnoll forces to harass Fire Temple.
"{@i Alrrem's fire burns brightly, but even the fiercest blaze leaves only ash and regret.}" — Romag

entries.name: Kelno of the Air Temple
entries: {@b Private Thoughts:} Romag sees Kelno as weak and indecisive—a leader easily manipulated but not an immediate threat. However, he remains cautious of Kelno's attempts to forge alliances with rivals.

{@b Strategy:} Exploits Kelno's desperation, offering occasional support in exchange for intelligence on the Fire and Water Temples. Romag has spies among Kelno's bugbears to monitor the Air Temple's movements.
"{@i Alrrem's fire burns brightly, but even the fiercest blaze leaves only ash and regret.}" - Romag

entries.name: Senshock Temple Wizard and Advisor
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Cautious respect

{@b Private Thoughts:} Romag acknowledges Senshock's magical prowess but deeply distrusts his loyalty to Iuz. He believes Senshock meddles too much in Temple politics and keeps a watchful eye on him for signs of treachery.

{@b Strategy:} Romag maintains a formal relationship, offering limited support while ensuring the Earth Temple remains self-sufficient in magical resources. He avoids direct confrontation but keeps Senshock at arm's length.

entries.name: Commander Barkinar, Temple Troops
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Courteous cooperation

{@b Private Thoughts:} Romag respects Barkinar's military discipline and sees potential for an alliance, though he remains wary of Barkinar's ambitions. Their shared appreciation for order makes Barkinar one of the few individuals Romag might genuinely trust.

{@b Strategy:} Romag cultivates a working relationship with Barkinar, offering logistical support while subtly steering him against Alrrem and Belsornig. He ensures the Earth Temple's forces remain disciplined and well-trained.

entries.name: Feldrin, Temple Security
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Mutual respect

{@b Private Thoughts:} Romag sees Feldrin as competent but inflexible, an obstacle rather than a true rival. Feldrin's unwavering loyalty to Hedrack makes him dangerous, as any overreach by Romag could be reported directly to the Supreme Commander.

{@b Strategy:} Romag keeps his dealings with Feldrin strictly professional, ensuring Earth Temple activities remain within Temple regulations. He takes advantage of Feldrin's respect for order, positioning himself as the most stable and reliable of the elemental leaders.

entries.name: Falrinth (Temple Diviner and Occultist)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Dismissive indifference

{@b Private Thoughts:} Romag considers Falrinth an eccentric nuisance, obsessed with arcane mysteries and largely disconnected from Temple politics. However, he recognizes that the wizard's knowledge could be useful under the right circumstances.

{@b Strategy:} Romag avoids direct engagement with Falrinth but keeps tabs on his research through intermediaries, ensuring he stays informed of any occult discoveries that might shift the Temple's balance of power.
"{@i The earth cares not for the mutterings of madmen.}" - Romag

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand Temple Assassin
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Cordial wariness

{@b Private Thoughts:} Romag recognizes Smigmal as a dangerous operative and possible informant for his rivals. He suspects she gathers intelligence for Falrinth or Belsornig and takes measures to shield critical Earth Temple operations from her scrutiny.

{@b Strategy:} Romag keeps Smigmal at a distance, offering occasional "gifts" to maintain a façade of goodwill while ensuring his most trusted guards remain vigilant in her presence. He is quietly considering employing assassins of his own to counter her influence if necessary.
"{@i A blade hidden in the dark is no match for stone walls and iron discipline.}"- Romag

entries.name: Tactics and Strategy
entries: The Cult of the Black Earth, under Romag's leadership, excels in defensive warfare and siege tactics. They employ monsters like bulettes and ankhegs to create subterranean strongholds and trap-laden passageways. Romag prefers to avoid direct confrontation, using the terrain and his followers' expertise to wear down foes.

entries.name: Tactical Strengths
entries: {@b Fortification:} Expert in defensive strongholds and underground networks.
{@b Ambush:} Uses terrain and tunnels to set traps.
{@b Endurance:} Prefers attrition-based tactics, outlasting enemies over prolonged engagements.

entries.name: Kelno, High Priest
entries: High Priest Kelno is the embittered and cunning leader of the Air Temple within the Temple of Elemental Evil. Once an ambitious cleric with grand designs, Kelno now stands as the sole survivor of his temple's leadership, his apprentices having been slain in the vicious power struggles between the elemental factions. Hailing from the lawless city of Narwell on The Wild Coast, Kelno brings a ruthless pragmatism and a keen eye for opportunity to the Temple's dark cause. He believes himself to be the true voice of Elemental Evil, despite his constant struggles against rival factions.

entries.name: Master of the Air Temple
entries: Kelno is a wiry Oeridian/Suel man in his late forties with sharp, hawk-like features and piercing blue eyes that reflect both intelligence and paranoia. His dark, thinning hair is always meticulously combed back, and he wears chain mail beneath flowing gray robes adorned with swirling air motifs. His demeanor is cold and calculating, his every word chosen with precision to further his hidden goals. He is rarely seen without his enchanted cloak of protection and his prized mace +1.

entries.name: Background
entries: Born in Narwell, a city known for its cunning thieves and ambitious bandits, Kelno grew up learning the value of alliances and betrayal. Drawn to the worship of the Elder Elemental Eye through whispered promises of power, he abandoned his previous faith in the Suel deity Norebo, god of luck and thieves, and joined the Temple's resurgence to seek his fortune.

items: Joined the Earth Temple initially but sought greater influence by shifting to the Air Temple.
Quickly rose through the ranks by undermining rivals and leveraging his connections in the Wild Coast to supply the Temple with mercenaries.
After the deaths of his apprentices at the hands of the Fire and Water Temples, Kelno has turned his focus to vengeance employing bugbears and gnolls.

entries.name: Personality
entries: Kelno is arrogant and fanatical, seeing himself as the true voice of Elemental Evil. He views the Elder Elemental God as an invisible storm, whispering chaos and destruction to those who will listen. Despite his confidence, he struggles with his diminishing influence and constant setbacks caused by rival factions. Kelno is a bitter, self-serving manipulator who thrives in the treacherous environment of the Temple. He has an intense hatred for both the Fire and Water Temples, blaming them for his setbacks, and is willing to bargain with anyone to see them suffer.
"{@i I care not for their blind faith—I seek power, and I will have it.}" - Kelno

entries.name: Prefect Kelno
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Cleric (Level 5), chaotic evil}
{@i Armor Class 18 (+1 chain mail, ring of protection)}
{@i Hit Points 32 (5d8 + 10)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Saving Throws Wis +5}
{@i Skills Deception +3, History +2, Religion +4}
{@i Senses passive Perception 13}
{@i Languages Common, Goblin}
{@i Challenge 3 (700 XP)}

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: Kelno is a 5th-level spellcaster (Wisdom, Spell Save DC 13, +5 to hit).
{@b Cantrips (at will):} {@spell light}, {@spell mending}, {@spell resistance}, {@spell thunderclap}
{@b 1st level (4 slots):} {@spell command}, {@spell cure wounds}, {@spell protection from evil and good}, {@spell sanctuary}
{@b 2nd level (3 slots):} {@spell blindness/deafness}, {@spell dust devil}, {@spell gust of wind}, {@spell skywrite}, {@spell warding wind}
{@b 3rd level (2 slots):} {@spell protection from energy}

entries.name: Special Equipment
entries: +1 Chain Mail (AC bonus included)
{@item Ring of Protection} (+1 to AC and saving throws)
+1 Ridged Mace (1d8+3 slashing)
{@item Dagger of Venom} (1d4+4 piercing, DC 15 Con save or take 2d10 poison and be poisoned for 1 min, once per day)

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Dagger of Venom.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 6 (1d4 + 4) piercing damage, DC 15 Con save or take 11 (2d10) poison damage and be poisoned for 1 min. (1/day)
{@b +1 Ridged Mace.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 7 (1d8 + 3) slashing damage.
{@b Deathly Wind.} {@i Reactions:} Kelno magically deflects a ranged attack made against him, pushing the attacker back 10 ft. if within 60 ft. (DC 13 Dex save or knocked prone).

entries.name: Role in the Temple
entries: Kelno commands the remnants of the Air Temple, maintaining his authority through bribery and mercenary contracts, primarily employing bugbears and gnolls. His quarters, though once opulent, now reflect his dwindling resources, as he exhausts his wealth in his relentless pursuit of power and vengeance. Despite these setbacks, Kelno remains a dangerous player, using deception and political maneuvering to maintain his position.

items: {@b Recruitment:} Maintains contacts in Narwell and Nulb to bring in additional forces to the Temple.
{@b Weather Manipulation:} Experiments with controlling weather patterns to disrupt enemies and instill fear.
{@b Espionage:} Uses spies within the Temple to monitor rival factions and report weaknesses.
{@b Survival Tactics:} Avoids direct combat, relying on hidden escape routes and misinformation.
{@b Political Maneuvering:} Feigns loyalty to Hedrack while exploiting internal conflicts to improve his standing.

entries.name: Political Relationships
entries: Kelno navigates the Temple's treacherous political landscape with cunning, forging temporary alliances while secretly plotting his own ascension.

items: {@b Hedrack (Supreme Commander):} Publicly loyal, but secretly despises his leadership and seeks to undermine him.
{@b Belsornig (Water Temple Leader):} His most dangerous rival; Kelno dreams of assassinating him.
{@b Alrrem (Fire Temple Leader):} Holds deep hatred for Alrrem, blaming him for past losses.
{@b Romag (Earth Temple Leader):} Considers him an easy pawn to manipulate in his schemes.
{@b Smigmal Redhand (Assassin Leader):} Regards her as a valuable but dangerous asset for covert operations.

entries: "{@i The winds of fate shift constantly; only fools stand against the storm.}" - Kelno

entries.name: Forces Under Kelno's Command
entries: {@b Kelno's Bodyguards}} – Personal enforcers and guards.

items: Bugbears (16): Heavy shock troops used for raids and ambushes.
Gnolls (2): Mercenaries, scouts skilled in tracking.
Goblins (4): Runners, spies, and messengers.
{@b Potential Reinforcements from the Water Temple}:
Bugbear Chieftain: Considering shifting allegiance.
Bugbear Guards (4): His personal bodyguards who may join the Air Temple ranks.

entries.name: Cult of the Howling Hatred (Air Temple)
entries: Kelno and his followers of the Cult of the Howling Hatred (Air Temple) believe the Elder Elemental God manifests as an invisible storm, whispering secrets of chaos and destruction. To them, air is the ultimate force, capable of slowly eroding civilization until only the raw power of the elements remains.

items: {@b Belief:} The Elder Elemental God exists as a whispering storm, an unseen force of devastation and entropy.
{@b Goal:} Control the skies and weather, summoning hurricanes and violent winds to weaken enemies and sow terror.
{@b Vengeance:} Destroy the Fire and Water Temples, avenging his slain apprentices.
{@b Rebuild Power:} Expand his forces by recruiting mercenaries and bribing key figures.
{@b Escape Plan:} Always prepared to flee if the Temple falls, ensuring his survival.

entries.name: Temple Politics
entries: Kelno is caught in the Temple's power struggles, constantly balancing alliances and betrayals.

items: {@b Belsornig (Water Temple Leader):} Seeks to manipulate humanoid forces away from the Air Temple by leveraging the Fire Temple's Alrrem as a looming threat.
{@b Fire vs. Air Conflict:} Kelno knows Belsornig is using Alrrem's aggression to destabilize him.
{@b Bugbear Loyalties:} The bugbear chieftain, wary of Belsornig's dealings with the trolls, has begun secret negotiations with Kelno.
{@b Troll Power Shift:} If Belsornig successfully secures the trolls' loyalty (Areas 203, 218-219), the bugbears (Areas 223-226) plan to defect en masse to the Air Temple.

entries.name: Alrrem, Prefect
entries: Prefect Alrrem is the cunning and ambitious leader of the Fire Temple, a once-powerful faction within the Temple of Elemental Evil. With his forces dwindling and influence waning, he is desperate to reclaim the former glory of his temple. Alrrem is known for his fiery temper, lavish appearance, and skill in political manipulation within the Temple's volatile hierarchy. His past is haunted by his betrayal of his own brother, Ashrem, an act that both secured his rise and now fuels his paranoia.

entries.name: Chief Cleric of the Fire Temple
entries: Alrrem is a middle-aged Oeridian with a commanding presence, his features hardened by years of struggle and betrayal. A stocky build, piercing dark eyes, and a closely trimmed beard. He wears a resplendent robe of deep crimson with purple lining and golden trim, adorned with garnet-inlaid fire symbols and skull motifs. A gold fire amulet set with a ruby rests on a chain around his neck, a potent symbol of his fiery devotion. In battle, he is armed with a hammer +2, his magical chain mail and shield providing ample protection.

items: {@b Ultimatums and Threats:} Uses threats and promises of rewards to coerce allies and undermine enemies.
{@b Defensive Positioning:} Relies on his guards and clerics to hold the line in combat.
{@b Desperate Measures:} Will flee or sacrifice allies if the Fire Temple's survival is at risk.
{@b Reclaim Fire Temple Glory:} Desperately seeks to restore the Fire Temple's prominence.
{@b Crush Rivals:} Aims to eliminate Belsornig and Kelno as threats to his dominance.
{@b Secure Earth Temple Alliance:} Attempts to manipulate Romag into supporting the Fire Temple.
Commander Varek Redflame (Level 5 Fighter): Fire Guards Leader, enforcer of Alrrem's will.
Werewolves (2): Savage enforcers used for ambushes and assassinations.
Bugbears (7): Shock troops deployed for ambushes and temple defense.
Thogran Ashmaw Half-Orc Cleric/Fighter (Level 3/3): Liaison to Bugbear Guards
Othrel the Blackened Curate (Level 4 Cleric): Ritual Specialist
Vrogg Flameskin Troll, Hydra Keeper (Level 6)

entries.name: Alrrem, Prefect
entries: {@i Medium humanoid (human), Cleric (Level 5), chaotic evil}
{@i Armor Class 21 (+2 chain mail, +1 shield)}
{@i Hit Points 42 (5d8 + 10)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Savings Throws Con +4, Wis +5}
{@i Skills Deception +4, Intimidation +4, Religion +2}
{@i Senses passive Perception 13}
{@i Languages Common, Goblin}
{@i Challenge 4 (1,100 XP)}

entries.name: Special Equipment
entries: +2 Chain Mail (AC bonus included), +1 Shield, +2 Warhammer (engraved with fire runes)

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Burning Eminence.} As a bonus action, Alrrem can expend a spell slot to make his melee weapon attacks deal an additional 10 (3d6) fire damage until the end of the turn. Expend a 2nd-level spell slot or higher, fire damage increases by 1d6 per level.
{@b Dark Devotion.} Alrrem has advantage on saving throws against being charmed or frightened.

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: Alrrem is a 5th-level spellcaster (Wisdom, Spell Save DC 13, +5 to hit).
{@b Cantrips (at will):} {@spell control Flames}, {@spell create bonfire}, {@spell light}, {@spell resistance}, {@spell sacred flame}, {@spell thaumaturgy}
{@b 1st level (4 slots):} {@spell command}, {@spell cure wounds}, {@spell sanctuary}
{@b 2nd level (3 slots):} {@spell blindness/deafness}, {@spell flaming arrows}, {@spell hold person}, {@spell pyrotechnics}, {@spell spiritual weapon}
{@b 3rd level (2 slots):} {@spell dispel magic}, {@spell flame walk}

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Multiattack.} Alrrem makes two melee weapon attacks.
{@b +2 Warhammer.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +5 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 7 (1d8 + 3) bludgeoning damage, or 8 (1d10 + 3) if wielded with two hands.
{@b Light Hammer.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +3 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 3 (1d4 + 1) bludgeoning damage.

entries.name: History and Background
entries: Alrrem and his brother Ashrem was born and raised in Dyvers, where he served as a lesser priest of the Oeridian goddess Sotillion, seeking wealth and influence. His brother Ashrem, a priest of Wenta, served alongside him. The two siblings were lured into the Temple of Elemental Evil by Hedrack, who appealed to their ambition through opulence and indulgence. Both brothers found themselves initiated into the Temple—Alrrem to the Fire Temple and Ashrem to the Air Temple, their natural inclinations guiding their choices.
Alrrem proved to be a natural leader within the Fire Temple, rising through the ranks quickly.
However, his leadership faltered in the aftermath of The Battle of Emridy Meadows, where the forces of the Temple were decisively defeated.

items: During the First Temple's fall, his faction led a disastrous offensive, leading to their decline in power.
He betrayed his brother Ashrem, offering him as a sacrifice to the Elder Elemental God, an act that cemented his position but left him emotionally scarred.
Alrrem's faction never fully recovered, and now he struggles to regain dominance by manipulating rival factions.

entries.name: The Betrayal of Ashrem
entries: As the Fire Temple rose in prominence, Alrrem saw an opportunity to consolidate power. Fearful of his brother's growing influence within the Air Temple, Alrrem conspired with his rivals and betrayed Ashrem, offering him as a sacrifice to the Elemental Nodes.

However, unknown to Alrrem, Ashrem survived the ordeal, using his magic to heal his wounds and escape into the labyrinthine depths of the nodes. Now trapped, Ashrem has become a vengeful figure lurking in the shadows, plotting against his brother and waiting for the moment to strike back. Though Alrrem publicly claims indifference, the knowledge of his brother's survival gnaws at his mind, driving him to increasing paranoia.

entries.name: Personality and Motivation
entries: Alrrem is a desperate and ambitious leader, fully aware that his temple's power is waning. He is both cunning and reckless, willing to take great risks to restore the Fire Temple's former glory.

items: {@b Manipulative Opportunist:} Seeks alliances and exploits weaknesses among his rivals.
{@b Loyal to Fire:} Believes in the destructive purity of elemental fire and the inevitable dominance of his temple.
{@b Vengeful and Resentful:} Holds grudges against those who have wronged him, especially the Water and Air Temples.

entries.name: Role in Temple Politics
entries: Alrrem, the embattled leader of the Fire Temple, is desperate to reclaim his faction's former glory. With fewer resources and declining influence, he relies on bluster, intimidation, and reckless ambition to assert himself. Alrrem considers himself the rightful ruler of the Temple of Elemental Evil, believing that fire's destructive power is the only true path to dominance. He views the other elemental factions with disdain, considering them weak and misguided. Despite his bravado, he is pragmatic enough to understand the need for temporary alliances.

entries.name: Supreme Commander Hedrack (Greater Temple Leader, Mouth of Iuz)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Resentful obedience
{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem despises Hedrack, believing that his obsession with Iuz has corrupted the Temple's original purpose. However, he outwardly swears loyalty, hoping to usurp him.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem feigns obedience while secretly working to gather enough strength to challenge Hedrack's leadership. He constantly undermines the Greater Temple's authority by stoking tensions between factions.

entries.name: Canon Belsornig (High Priest of the Water Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Open hostility and rivalry
{@b Private Thoughts:} Belsornig's Alrrem's greatest rival and the biggest obstacle to his ambitions. Alrrem sees the Water Temple's influence as an insidious threat that must be eradicated.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem has launched multiple sabotage attempts against the Water Temple, seeking to weaken Belsornig's forces and credibility. He openly antagonizes him in meetings and commands his minions to spread misinformation about Water Temple corruption.

entries.name: Romag (High Priest of the Earth Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Scornful dismissal

{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem views Romag as a slow and ponderous fool, too concerned with fortifications and patience to be a real threat. However, he secretly fears the Earth Temple's steady growth.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem attempts to lure Romag into rash actions by provoking him through minor skirmishes and insults. He hopes to force the Earth Temple into conflict with the Water Temple, weakening both while Fire regains its power.
"{@i Romag thinks his stones will last forever, but even the mightiest mountain can be reduced to ash.}" - Alrrem

entries.name: Kelno (High Priest of the Air Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Contempt and condescension

{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem sees Kelno as a spineless opportunist, incapable of real leadership. He does not consider the Air Temple a threat but remains wary of Kelno's willingness to switch allegiances.
{@b Strategy:} Alrrem uses Kelno as a pawn, feeding him false promises of alliances while secretly undermining him. He encourages Kelno's paranoia, ensuring the Air Temple remains too weak.

entries.name: Senshock (Temple Wizard and Advisor to Hedrack)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Tenuous cooperation
{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem respects Senshock's magical power but finds him insufferable and meddlesome. He believes the wizard's loyalty to Hedrack makes him dangerous.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem works to keep Senshock occupied with petty intrigues and false leads, preventing him from focusing on the Fire Temple's activities. He seeks ways to counter Senshock's influence by recruiting his own spellcasters.

entries.name: Commander Barkinar (Commander of the Greater Temple Troops)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Mutual distrust

{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem sees Barkinar as a dangerous rival and a potential challenger to his position. Barkinar's growing influence over the Temple's military forces threatens Alrrem's fragile grip on power.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem attempts to weaken Barkinar by undermining his relationships with other factions, particularly through bribing the mercenaries under his command. He seeks to turn the rank-and-file troops against Barkinar with promises of wealth and glory under the Fire Temple.
"{@i Barkinar plays the long game, but he'll find that my fire consumes everything in time.}" - Alrrem

entries.name: Feldrin (Commander of Temple Security)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Respectful cooperation
{@b Private Thoughts:} Feldrin's loyalty to Hedrack makes him a dangerous obstacle, but Alrrem respects his tactical mind and military discipline.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem treats Feldrin with respect and provides logistical support in an attempt to gain his trust. He hopes to one day sway Feldrin's loyalty away from Hedrack and toward his own cause.

entries.name: Falrinth (Temple Diviner and Occultist)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Disdainful amusement
{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem considers Falrinth an eccentric fool obsessed with ancient mysteries. However, he fears the wizard's divinations may expose his secret dealings.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem keeps his distance from Falrinth, ensuring his plans remain hidden. He occasionally supplies him with trinkets and false information to keep him distracted. "{@i Falrinth searches for answers in the dirt. Let him, while I shape the future in fire.}"

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand (Temple Assassin and Spy, Lover of Falrinth)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Distrustful neutrality
{@b Private Thoughts:} Alrrem recognizes Smigmal as a dangerous wildcard who could shift alliances at any moment. He knows she is gathering intelligence on behalf of Falrinth and possibly others.

{@b Strategy:} Alrrem keeps Smigmal at arm's length, offering her occasional bribes and missions to keep her from turning against him. He is always wary of her presence and has contingency plans in place to eliminate her if necessary. "{@i Smigmal is a viper; useful, but you'd best not turn your back on her.}"

entries.name: Ashrem (Former Air Temple Leader)
entries: Alrrem's betrayal of his brother, leading to Ashrem's sacrifice, haunts him to this day. A dark specter from Alrrem's past; though believed dead, his brother's survival weighs heavily on his mind.

items: Sending ultimatums to the Earth Temple, offering rewards for their allegiance.
Secretly colluding with mercenaries and bandits in Nulb to increase his forces.
Planting false rumors to cause dissension within rival temples.

entries.name: Prefect Alrrem Written Message to High Priest Romag of the Earth Temple
entries: "Your rewards will be great if you bend your knee to Fire. Bring all of your forces here to me now, and swear fealty. If you fail to heed this advice…"

entries.name: Alrrem's Final Words
entries: "{@i Hedrack is blind to the Temple's true purpose. Fire will purge his corruption soon enough.}"- Alrrem

entries.name: Belsornig, Canon
entries: A master of political intrigue and manipulation, Canon Belsornig is the cunning and ambitious High Priest of the Water Temple within the Temple of Elemental Evil. Unlike his more reckless counterparts, Belsornig carefully orchestrates his rise to power, working through a vast network of spies and agents both inside the Temple and in Nulb. His ultimate goal is nothing short of domination, reshaping the Temple in his own vision of destruction and rebirth through water.

entries.name: Appearance and Personality
entries: {@b Appearance:} A wiry, middle-aged Oeridian/Suel with an aquiline nose and piercing blue eyes that seem to see through any deception. His silver-threaded black hair is slicked back, and his robes, embroidered with aquatic motifs, reflect his devotion to the power of water.

{@b Personality:} Arrogant and manipulative, Belsornig delights in outsmarting his enemies and peers. He thrives on chaos and views the struggles within the Temple as a proving ground for his superiority. While capable of extreme cruelty, he can feign kindness to manipulate those around him.

entries.name: Background
entries: Born in Hardby, near the Woolly Bay, Belsornig spent years navigating political intrigue in his homeland before being drawn to the Temple of Elemental Evil. He saw in the Water Temple an opportunity to wield true power, quickly climbing the ranks by exploiting rival factions and eliminating threats with surgical precision.

items: {@b Rise to Power:} Gained favor with the Greater Temple leadership by delivering key victories, solidifying his position as head of the Water Temple.
{@b Philosophy:} Believes the world must be reshaped through destruction and cleansing floods, aligning his vision with the power of the elemental forces he serves.

entries.name: Role in the Temple
entries: As High Priest of the Water Temple, Belsornig commands a powerful faction, using charisma and cunning to expand his influence.

items: {@b Leadership}} – A dangerous rival within the Temple, he seeks to dominate the other elemental factions.
{@b Factional Politics}} – Undermines the Air and Fire Temples while courting an alliance with Romag of the Earth Temple.
{@b Strategic Alliances}} – Feigns cooperation with the Fire Temple to weaken the Earth Temple's position.

entries.name: Canon Belsornig
entries: {@i Medium Humanoid (Human), Cleric level 6, Chaotic Evil}
{@i Armor Class 20 (+1 plate, Dual Wielder)}
{@i Hit Points 45 (6d8 + 18)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Saving Throws Dex +7, Wis +6}
{@i Condition Immunities Paralyzed & Restrained (Ring of Free Action)}
{@i Senses passive Perception 16}
{@i Languages Common, Giant, Goblin}
{@i Challenge 5 (1,800 XP)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Dark Devotion.} Advantage on saving throws against being Charmed or Frightened.
{@b Dual Wielder.} +1 AC while wielding two melee weapons; can draw or stow two one-handed weapons per turn.
{@b Virulent Strike.} As a bonus action, Belsornig may expend a spell slot to deal 4d6 necrotic damage on melee hits until the end of his turn.

entries.name: Spellcasting
entries: 6th-level spellcaster (Wisdom, DC 14, +6 to hit)
{@b Cantrips (at will):} {@spell Light}, {@spell Resistance}, {@spell Frostbite}, {@spell Shape Water}
{@b 1st (4/day):} {@spell Command}, {@spell Cure Wounds}, {@spell Ice Knife}
{@b 2nd (3/day):} {@spell Hold Person}, {@spell Prayer of Healing}, {@spell Silence}, {@spell Spiritual Weapon}
{@b 3rd (3/day):} {@spell Dispel Magic}, {@spell Wall of Water}

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Multiattack.} Belsornig makes two melee attacks, one with each weapon.

{@b Mace of Smiting.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +6 to hit (+8 vs. constructs), 5 ft. range. {@i Hit:} 6 (1d6 + 3) bludgeoning, or 8 (1d6 + 5) vs. constructs. {@i Crit:} +7 bludgeoning, or +14 vs. constructs. Destroys constructs with 25 HP or less.
{@b Mace.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} 5 to hit, 5 ft. range. {@i Hit:} 5 (1d6 + 2) bludgeoning.

entries.name: Relationships
entries: Belsornig maintains a facade of loyalty to the Greater Temple but secretly plots to usurp its leadership, believing that the power of elemental water is the key to dominance. He views his rivals within the Temple as obstacles to be manipulated or removed and uses his extensive network of agents to further his ambitions. His alliances are based solely on pragmatism, and he will betray anyone if it serves his interests.

entries.name: Hedrack (Supreme Commander)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Feigned loyalty
{@b Private Thoughts:} Sees Hedrack as an overindulgent ruler whose distractions leave the Temple vulnerable to internal coups.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig remains outwardly deferential, as a loyal supporter of the Greater Temple's goals. In truth, he works behind the scenes to weaken Hedrack's influence, seeking to replace him. He uses agents within the Greater Temple to sow discord and ensure that Hedrack remains distracted.

entries.name: Romag (High Priest of the Earth Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Pretends to consider an alliance
{@b Private Thoughts:} Views Romag as a narrow-minded zealot who lacks the vision to wield true power.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig manipulates Romag by offering false promises of alliance against Kelno and Alrrem while ensuring the Earth Temple remains too weak to act independently. He uses Romag's desire for stability while keeping him reliant on his resources.

entries.name: Kelno (High Priest of the Air Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Dominating pressure and threats
{@b Private Thoughts:} Considers Kelno weak and easily manipulated; views him as a tool against Alrrem.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig enjoys intimidating Kelno, using both political maneuvering and military pressure to keep the Air Temple subservient. He encourages Kelno's paranoia by planting false information about threats from Alrrem. Also bribing his bugbears.

entries.name: Alrrem (High Priest of the Fire Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Open hostility and political threat.
{@b Private Thoughts:} Views Alrrem as his most dangerous rival and actively seeks to destroy him.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig works to subvert Alrrem's remaining forces, spreading dissent among his troops and funding spies. To turn Alrrem's own bugbear mercenarie with promises of greater wealth. If given the opportunity, Belsornig will not hesitate to expose Alrrem's weaknesses to Hedrack.

entries.name: Senshock (Lord Wizard of the Greater Temple)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Polite but distant cooperation
{@b Private Thoughts:} Recognizes Senshock's value but distrusts him deeply.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig carefully avoids confrontation with Senshock, instead seeking to use the wizard's desire for power against him. He provides misinformation to keep Senshock focused on other targets. He knows Senshock's loyalty is to power alone, and he would strike a deal.

entries.name: Falrinth (Temple Diviner and Occultist)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Cordial but dismissive
{@b Private Thoughts:} Considers Falrinth a madman.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig largely ignores Falrinth, viewing him as obsessed with arcane mysteries. He keeps an eye on Falrinth's discoveries, recognizing that the wizard's obsession with forbidden knowledge could eventually produce something valuable.

entries.name: Smigmal Redhand (Temple Assassin and Spy, Lover of Falrinth)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Cautious neutrality
{@b Private Thoughts:} Views Smigmal as an unpredictable threat and potential asset.

{@b Strategy:} Belsornig regards Smigmal with a mix of wariness and intrigue. As Falrinth's lover, she holds influence over the enigmatic diviner, making her a valuable pawn. He has approached Smigmal with offers of wealth and power in an attempt to turn her against her lover. However, he remains cautious, knowing that she is a deadly assassin.

entries.name: Commander Barkinar (Temple Troops Commander)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} A necessary ally
{@b Private Thoughts:} Sees Barkinar as a dangerous yet pragmatic soldier who can be swayed.
{@b Strategy:} Belsornig keeps Barkinar close, offering tactical resources in exchange for loyalty. He understands that Barkinar ultimately serves whoever offers the greatest advantage.

entries.name: Commander Feldrin (Sub-Level Security Chief)
entries: {@b Public Stance:} Contemptuous but professional
{@b Private Thoughts:} Considers Feldrin a threat, given his deep loyalty to Hedrack.
{@b Strategy:} Belsornig takes care to avoid open conflict with Feldrin.

entries.name: Gefreid Patris
entries: Gefreid Patris is the ruthless overseer stationed at the Hunting Cottage, a critical logistical hub for the Temple of Elemental Evil. Directly appointed by Commander Feldrin, he supervises the covert flow of prisoners, supplies, and stolen goods between Nulb and the Greater Temple through a secret, well-guarded tunnel. Cold, calculating, and brutally pragmatic, Gefreid ensures his operations run smoothly, tolerating no errors from his handpicked bandits.
"{@i This cottage is just a hunting lodge, nothing more. You'd best turn back before you see something you shouldn't.}" - Gefreid

items: {@b Manipulative:} Quick to exploit weaknesses and instill fear in his subordinates.
{@b Pragmatic:} Focused on results, Gefreid values efficiency above loyalty, and is willing to make sacrifices for the success of his operations.
{@b Callous:} Treats prisoners and slaves as disposable resources, showing no empathy or remorse.
{@b Isolationist:} Prefers remote locales like the Gnarley Forest, away from prying eyes and meddling outsiders.

entries.name: Behaviors

items: Lets his lieutenant handle most interactions with strangers, appearing as a humble hunter when needed to maintain secrecy.
Ensures patrols and defensive snares surround the cottage and barn, maintaining vigilant perimeter.
Uses deceptive hospitality to delay or mislead outsiders who approach the cottage.

entries: "{@i I don't like to kill for fun. But if you force my hand, I won't lose sleep over it.}" - Gefreid

entries.name: Background and Temple Operations
entries: Originally a member of a mercenary company, Gefreid was cast out for his excessive brutality. Recruited into the Temple of Elemental Evil by Feldrin, he found his calling overseeing the Hunting Cottage's operations.

items: {@b Assignment:} Stationed at the Hunting Cottage, Gefreid views his role as both a privilege and an opportunity to profit from the Temple's supply chain.
{@b Managing Supply Chains:} Receiving shipments of prisoners, supplies, and loot from bandits and pirates.
{@b Enforcing Order:} Keeping greater temple guards and bandit mercenaries disciplined and focused.
{@b Maintaining Secrecy:} Ensuring no outsiders discover the covert operations leading to the Greater Temple.
{@b Slavery Logistics:} Gefreid coordinates closely with Madame Selentis and her men, an agent of Hedrack to bring prisoners from the Boatman's Tavern and Wainwright of Nulb to the Cottage.

entries.name: Gefreid Patris
entries: {@i Medium Humanoid, Chaotic Evil, Fighter 4}
{@i Armor Class 16 (Scale Mail, Shield, Ring of Protection +1)}
{@i Hit Points 27 (4d10+4)}
{@i Speed 30ft.}
{@i Saving Throws Str +3, Con +3}
{@i Skills Intimidation +2, Survival +2}
{@i Senses passive Perception 9}
{@i Languages Common}
{@i Challenge 3 (700 XP)}

entries.name: Traits
entries: {@b Fighting Style (Dueling).} When wielding a melee weapon in one hand, Gefreid gains +2 to damage rolls (in the stat block).
{@b Second Wind (1/Short Rest).} As a bonus action, Gefreid regains {@dice 1d10+4} HP.
{@b Action Surge (1/Short Rest).} On his turn, he can take an extra action, allowing for additional attacks or movement.

entries.name: Actions
entries: {@b Multiattack.} Gefreid makes two melee attacks.
{@b Masterwork Hand Axes.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. or range 20/60 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 9 (1d6+5) slashing damage (includes Dueling Fighting Style bonus).
{@b Longbow.} {@i Melee Weapon Attack:} +2 to hit, range 150/600 ft., one target. {@i Hit:} 4 (1d8) piercing damage.
{@b Brutal Opportunist.} If Gefreid attacks a creature that is flanked or unaware, he deals an additional 4 (1d8) damage once per turn.

entries.name: Expanded Greater Temple Supply Operations
entries: Gefreid Patris plays a crucial role in the logistical network of the Temple of Elemental Evil, acting as a central hub for supplies, prisoners, and stolen goods moving between the Moathouse, Nulb, and the Temple itself. His management of the Hunting Cottage positions him as an indispensable facilitator of the Temple's covert operations.

entries.name: Coordination with Gattas Void at the Moathouse
entries: Gefreid maintains regular and discreet contact with Lieutenant Gattas Void, second-in-command to Lareth at the Moathouse High Road Bandit Camp. Gattas coordinates frequent raids against merchant caravans, isolated farms, and travelers throughout the Viscounty of Verbobonc. Captured livestock, valuable loot, and abducted prisoners are transferred by heavily guarded wagons to Gefreid's custody at the Hunting Cottage.

Upon receiving shipments, Gefreid personally inspects the captured goods and evaluates prisoners. Valuable captives suitable for ritual sacrifice or forced labor are transferred directly into the tunnel network toward the Greater Temple. Supplies and lesser prisoners are redirected to Madame Selentis's operatives in Nulb for secondary distribution.

entries.name: Gefreid's Bandits (Greater Temple)
entries: Gefreid commands a disciplined squad of hardened Temple-aligned brigands provided by Commander Deggum. These human bandits maintain the secrecy and efficiency of operations, enforcing Feldrin's orders.

entries.name: Bandit Personnel Roster
entries: {@b Morvik Blackthorn (Lieutenant)} – Second-in-command; cautious, organized, and fiercely loyal to Gefreid. Responsible for day-to-day discipline.
{@b Jarus Vrell} – Silent tracker and scout; known for detecting threats long before they reach the cottage perimeter.
{@b Dieter "Redknife"} – Former pirate turned enforcer; infamous for his intimidation skills and handling prisoner transfers.
{@b Gavrik "Ironfoot"} – Manages barn operations; tough, burly, and methodical. Oversees packing, repacking, and prisoner containment.
{@b Elric the Younger} – Youngest bandit; ambitious and watchful. Reports on any irregularities among his peers to Gefreid directly.
{@b Ralf "The Hound"} – Bandit tasked with resetting perimeter snares each morning; brutal and quick to violence if triggered.
{@b Quillen} – In charge of courier horses and supply-wagon handling; meticulous with shipment logs and inventory checks.
{@b Brannoc Grimtooth} – Prisoner overseer; cruel and unfeeling, enjoys authority over captives awaiting transport.

entries.name: Vulnerability of the Tunnel
entries: Though well-hidden, the supply tunnel is a single point of failure in the Greater Temple's operations. Adventurers who discover and sabotage the tunnel could cut off the Temple's lifeline. (T1-4 TOEE module page 38, 4. ESCAPE TUNNEL)

entries.name: Logistical Operations
entries: Under Gefreid's careful oversight, the Hunting Cottage serves multiple critical roles:

items: {@b Command Post}: Coordination of logistical operations, prisoner sorting, and intelligence exchange.
{@b Supply Depot}: Receiving shipments of stolen goods, prisoners, and supplies from Madame Selentis's bandits, Vesrek's pirates, and Gattas Void's raiders.
{@b Prisoner Handling}: Slaves and prisoners destined for sacrifice or labor in the Temple are temporarily held and sorted in reinforced barn cages.

entries.name: Political Relationships
entries: {@b Commander Feldrin}: Gefreid answers directly to Feldrin, the Temple supply commander.
{@b Lucius Graeme}: Regularly inspects operations and delivers orders. Gefreid respects Lucius's position but finds him insufferably arrogant.
{@b Madame Selentis (Nysera Krivaltis)}: Exchanges shipments through intermediaries. Distrustful of her ambitions but acknowledges her ruthless efficiency.
{@b Smigmal Redhand and The Crimson Reapers}: Occasionally delivers shipments directly, maintaining tension and wary cooperation.
{@b Gattas Void (Lareth's Lieutenant)}: Supplies regular deliveries of prisoners and loot from the Moathouse. Their relationship is strictly professional and transactional.

data.name: 55. Hunting Cottage and Barn
entries: The hunting cottage is a vital logistical node for the operations of the Temple of Elemental Evil. Located south of Nulb and near the Imeryd's Run, this unassuming log structure masks a sinister purpose: it serves as a supply depot, a slave-handling station, and a gateway into the Temple itself. Hidden deep within the Gnarley Forest, it is a place of grim purpose and dark machinations, overseen by loyal agents of the Greater Temple and guarded by Deggum's men.

items: {@b Structure:} A rustic log hunting Cottage and barn with a simple but sturdy construction, sitting near the ford of a meandering stream.
{@b Water Well:} Weed and algae crusted water well.
{@b Surroundings:} Dense woods encircle the Cottage, though a wide, well-worn path leads directly to it, bearing wagon ruts from regular supply deliveries.

entries.name: Hidden Tunnel Operations
entries: The tunnel connects directly to the Greater Temple's Broken Tower. Most of the traffic includes essential goods, weapons, and slaves, ensuring the Temple remains stocked without raising suspicion in Nulb.

entries.name: Deadly Traps
entries: Deadly traps concealed within thick underbrush surrounding the cottage and barn.
Carefully reset daily by Ralf and rotating patrol bandits, ensuring constant vigilance.

entries.name: Dire Hounds
entries: Three ferocious dire hounds patrol the immediate cottage grounds.
Trained to respond solely to Gefreid's commands, attacking intruders without hesitation.

entries.name: Tunnel Security
entries: The concealed tunnel entrance is constantly guarded by pairs of well-armed bandits, rotating shifts every few hours.
Rune-marked alarm stones linked directly to Temple spellcasters alert Gefreid and Feldrin to intrusion attempts.

entries.name: Natural Cave Outpost (Bugbear & Goblin Guards)
entries: Midway along the tunnel, a recently excavated passage connects to a natural cave serving as a fortified checkpoint.
A band of disciplined {@creature Bugbear}s and {@creature Goblin}s under Temple command monitor this critical junction, demanding passwords or tokens of authority from passing personnel.

entries.name: Guarded Water Well (Giant Rats)
entries: The secret tunnel ultimately terminates near an old, stone-lined water well deep below the Greater Temple complex.
This final passage is infested with aggressive {@creature Giant Rat}s purposefully bred and released to attack intruders and deter unauthorized access.

entries.name: Recruitment Operations
entries: The Temple of Elemental Evil is more than just a bastion of darkness—it is a growing army, meticulously built through recruitment efforts that span the region. From river pirates and mercenaries to monstrous warbands, the Temple ensures a steady influx of warriors, spellcasters, and creatures to bolster its ranks. Each elemental faction—Earth, Fire, Water, and Air—has distinct recruitment strategies, ensuring the Temple remains a constant threat to the forces of good.

entries.name: Recruitment by Faction
entries: Each elemental faction within the Temple maintains its own recruitment network, leveraging unique strategies:

items: {@b Earth Temple (Romag):} Uses blackmail, intimidation, and coercion to recruit bandits, pirates, and mercenaries from Nulb and Dyvers. Works closely with Gorvash the Cruel of the Iron Claw Clan (Bugbears) and King Korvash Bonegnaw of the Jagged Fang Tribe (Gnolls)—alliances originally secured by Lareth the Beautiful.
{@b Fire Temple (Alrrem):} Attracts fanatics, pyromaniacs, and warlike humanoids, drawing in werewolves, bugbears, and trolls from the fringes.

{@b Water Temple (Belsornig):} Uses bribery and deception to recruit river pirates, smugglers, and mercenaries. Works with Captain Garik Marath, a smuggler of the Cult of the Crushing Wave, who supplies the Temple with slaves and stolen goods.
{@b Air Temple (Kelno):} Appeals to assassins, spies, and shadow operatives, attracting those who value stealth and subterfuge. This faction specializes in tracking, sabotage, and high-risk missions.

entries.name: Key Recruiting Agents
entries: Many of the Temple's leaders play an active role in recruitment, ensuring its constant expansion:

items: {@b Commander Feldrin:} Oversees recruitment of bandits, mercenaries, and supply convoys to maintain the Temple's forces.
{@b Commander Barkinar:} Expands Temple forces through organized raids and manages the Gnolls of the Iron Claw Clan and Jagged Fang Tribe.
{@b Commander Deggum:} Oversees ogres and Temple guards, ensuring defensive readiness and recruiting the Blackthorn Tribe (Ogres, Bugbears, and Hobgoblins).

{@b Dick Rentsch (Nulb):} Owner of the Waterside Hostel, Dick actively recruits cutthroats and informants on behalf of Romag and the Earth Temple. Carefully bolstering Romag's covert influence within Nulb.
{@b Lareth (Moathouse):} Lareth recruits brigands, mercenaries, and exiled outlaws, building his own bandit forces. He balances devotion to Lolth with pragmatic alliance to the Temple's resurgence.
{@b Lucius Graeme:} Travels between Nulb, Hommlet, and Verbobonc, recruiting informants and spies.

{@b Smigmal Redhand:} Leads surface defenses and raiding parties, allowing only the most ruthless warriors entry into the Temple. Uses her magical hat to maintain a double identity as an elven diplomat, gathering intelligence from Nulb and Hommlet.

{@b Madame Selentis (Old Mayor's Manor, Nulb):} Serves as Hedrack's primary logistical overseer in Nulb. She commands a disciplined and loyal band of Temple-aligned bandits assigned by Smigmal Redhand, overseeing smuggling operations, prisoner trafficking, and resource distribution to the Temple.

{@b Skole (Boatman's Tavern):} A former riverman turned smuggler and criminal enforcer, Skole manages black-market trade under Lodriss's watchful eye, coordinates meetings and illicit dealings between river pirates, smugglers, and Temple agents.
{@b Lodriss (Boatman's Tavern):} Proprietor of the Boatman's Tavern, Lodriss is the true owner. A shrewd and ambitious former camp follower, Lodriss has a firm grasp on Nulb's underworld.
{@b Captain Marad Vesrek:} Commands the slaver ship The Crimson Trader hailing from the Wild Coast, supplying the Temple with slaves.
{@b Captain Tolub (Boatman's Tavern):} Agent of the Water Temple. Commands the pirate ship Iron Dagger, providing Belsornig with supplies.
{@b Master Rom (Waterside Hostel):} Mage and Commander of the Crimson Reapers. Brigands newly arrived from Dyvers.

data.name: The Temple’s Strategic Recruitment in Nulb

entries.name: The Temple's Strategic Recruitment in Nulb
entries: Nulb serves as a crucial hub for covert recruitment and logistics:

items: {@b Covert Recruitment:} Pirates, outlaws, and cutthroats are drawn in through coin, weapons, and protection.
{@b Resource Smuggling:} The Nulb docks act as a major transfer point for weapons, supplies, and slaves.
{@b Boatman's Tavern & Nulb Market:} A center for black-market trade and recruitment.
{@b Hunting Cottage (secret tunnel):} Gefreid Patris overseer of operations provides hidden access to the Temple for supplies, slaves and new recruits.
{@b Captain Tolub & Captain Vesrek:} Facilitate the transportation of goods and personnel.
{@b Ruined Tower (Smigmal Redhand):} The Temple's surface fortress, ensuring only recruits gain access.

entries.name: Recruitment Sources
entries: The Temple actively seeks new followers from multiple backgrounds:

items: {@b Bandits & Brigands:} The Crimson Reapers and other gangs provide a constant influx of raiders.
{@b Mercenaries & Cutthroats:} Recruited from Dyvers, Narwell, and the Wild Coast, drawn by gold and power.
{@b Humanoids:} Gnolls, bugbears, goblins, and ogres join for gold, plunder, or religious fervor.
{@b Monsters:} Werewolves, trolls, and elementals are brought into the fold through dark rituals and demonic pacts.
Low-level humanoids (1 HD or less): 1 per day per faction (7 total daily).
Higher-level humanoids (3 HD+): 1 per faction every three days.
Monsters: 1 per faction every week.
196 low-level humanoids
70 high-level humanoids
28 monsters

entries.name: The Temple's Growing Threat
entries: If left unchecked, the Temple of Elemental Evil will continue its unchecked expansion, bolstered by its relentless recruitment machine. Raids, assassinations, and growing alliances with monstrous factions will threaten Hommlet, Verbobonc, and beyond.

entries.name: Monstrous Recruitment
entries: The Temple relies on key leaders to forge alliances with monstrous humanoid warbands:

items: Commander Deggum → Blackthorn Tribe (Eastern Gnarley Forest - Ogres & Monstrous Humanoids)
Commander Feldrin → Iron Claw Bugbears (Southern Gnarley Forest - Mercenary Warbands)
Lareth → Jagged Fang Gnolls (Lortmil Mountains)

entries.name: Blackthorn Tribe (Gnarley Forest)
entries: Deggum is in charge of maintaining security and defense within the Temple, including recruitment of monstrous allies. His established ties with Chieftain Garghuk of the Blackthorn Tribe in the eastern Gnarley. As Commander he has the authority and capability to negotiate with the Blackthorn Tribe, which consists of monstrous humanoids like ogres, orcs, and goblinoids. His military-focused role means he would actively seek strong foot soldiers for the Temple.

entries.name: Iron Claw Clan (Welkwood Forest)
entries: Feldrin is responsible for the Temple's bandits, raiders, and mercenary forces, making him the perfect liaison for the Iron Claw Clan, led by Warlord Braggok Iron-Claw in Varifane, which consists of ruthless bugbear warbands known for their guerrilla tactics and raiding. Bugbears respect discipline and organization, and Feldrin's structured command appeals to their desire for raids and plunder.

entries.name: Jagged Fang Tribe (Lortmil Mountains)
entries: Lareth is a charismatic and cunning recruiter who excels in persuading warlike and chaotic creatures, recruited gnolls—a race notorious for their bloodthirsty, brutal, and savage tendencies. King Korvash Bonegnaw of the Jagged Fang Gnoll Tribe operates in the Lortmil Mountains. Lareth's devotion to the Elder Elemental God and Lolth's influence aligns well with the gnoll's taste for slaughter and chaos, making them eager allies.

data.name: QUESTS EXPANDED CAMPAIGN
entries: Verbobonc 576cy

data.name: Hedrack - Breaking the Chain of Command

entries.name: Hedrack - Breaking the Chain of Command
entries: Adventurers uncover that Hedrack, the Supreme Commander of the Temple of Elemental Evil and Mouth of Iuz, is orchestrating a sinister agenda: preparing the Temple for a devastating invasion of Furyondy and Veluna, while secretly pursuing the liberation of the demon queen Zuggtmoy. Hedrack masterfully maintains authority through ruthless intimidation, strategic manipulation, and precarious alliances, all while covertly serving the dual interests of Iuz and the EEG.

items: Confront Hedrack: Infiltrate the Greater Temple and defeat Hedrack to dismantle the leadership.
Uncover His Plans: Defeat Hedrack, neutralizing his direct command over Temple forces.

Sabotage the Temple's Operations: Sever critical supply lines managed by Commander Feldrin, Gefreid Patris, and Madame Selentis. Intercept and disrupt slave shipments orchestrated by pirates like Captain Vesrek and bandits led by Gattas Void.
Leverage Temple Politics: Encourage factional rivalry between elemental prophets Romag, Belsornig, Alrrem, and Kelno to weaken the Temple.
Exploit Internal Divisions: Leverage ambitions and internal conflicts among powerful lieutenants such as Barkinar, Deggum, and Senshock.
Madame Selentis (Nysera Krivaltis): Civilian proxy in Nulb, ambitious yet cautiously loyal. Her resentment of Hedrack's control may make her open to betrayal.
Lucius Graeme: Temple courier and intelligence officer, deeply knowledgeable of Hedrack's plans and weaknesses; could be a powerful double agent.
Smigmal Redhand and Falrinth: Surface defense commanders and guardians of the Orb of Golden Death. Secretly aligned with Lolth.
Romag and Belsornig: Prophets with vested interests in Hedrack's downfall; their rivalry could be leveraged to create internal chaos.

entries.name: Aftermath and Consequences
entries: Hedrack's death immediately fractures the Temple hierarchy, sparking power struggles.
Increased regional instability, leading to intensified raids, violent reprisals, and greater vigilance.

entries.name: Long-Term Ramifications
entries: Surviving lieutenants, particularly Feldrin, Deggum, Barkinar, and Madame Selentis, may seize new positions of power, creating new threats or potential alliances. Lolth-aligned factions, led by Falrinth, Smigmal, and Lareth, could take advantage of the chaos and strengthen Lolth's presence.

entries.name: Senshock - The Arcane Ascendancy
entries: Senshock, the Lord Wizard of the Temple of Elemental Evil, is obsessed with unlocking the full power of the Orb of Golden Death and the Elemental Nodes. With Zuggtmoy trapped and Iuz exerting control over the Temple, Senshock seeks ultimate power through mastering the Orb. However, to do so, he needs to recover the Elemental Power Gems hidden within the Elemental Nodes.
The players can either aid or oppose Senshock in his goal—either securing the gems for their own purposes, preventing his ascension, or attempting to destroy the Orb.

The Orb of Golden Death (or Yellowskull) is the heart of Senshock's plan to seize ultimate power within the Temple. However, the rogue wizard Falrinth already possesses the artifact, keeping it hidden from the Greater Temple for his own ends. Senshock does not know this, Hedrack assumes the Orb was lost long ago.
The players enter a dangerous web of intrigue, deceit, and power struggles, where Senshock, Falrinth, and even Zuggtmoy and Iuz all seek the legendary artifact.

items: A Desperate Summons: Senshock, suspecting that the Orb has resurfaced, enlists the players to track it down, promising wealth, magical boons, or power within the Temple if they deliver it to him.
A Whisper in the Shadows: Falrinth, operating in secrecy, contacts the players via his quasit familiar, Kriitch. He offers them knowledge of the Orb's power—if they help him secure the gems first.

An Ancient Prophecy: An elven sage or captured prisoner within the Temple reveals that the Orb's full power can only be realized when all four Elemental Gems are restored—but that to do so will risk awakening an even greater evil.
The Will of Zuggtmoy: If the players have encountered Zuggtmoy's influence, she may appear in dreams or visions, demanding they retrieve the Orb for her cultists—with the promise of rewards.

entries.name: Act 1: A Race Against Time
entries: The players must locate the Orb—but do they deliver it to Senshock, Falrinth, or keep it for themselves?

entries.name: Senshock's Offer
entries: Senshock believes Hedrack's power must be challenged and sees the Orb as a way to control the Elemental Nodes before his rivals.
He instructs the players to locate Falrinth's lair (Area 337) and retrieve the Orb at any cost.
If they succeed, Senshock will send them into the Elemental Nodes to claim the four Elemental Gems, making them his elite champions.

entries.name: Falrinth's Deception
entries: Falrinth, a servant of Lolth, views the Orb as his ultimate bargaining chip. He tells the players that Senshock is a fool and that Hedrack and Zuggtmoy must be overthrown.
He offers them access to his Escape Tunnel—but only if they agree to work with him.
If the players betray Senshock and side with Falrinth, they must safeguard the Orb from the Temple's leadership, ensuring neither Zuggtmoy nor Iuz can sense its location.

entries.name: The Third Path
entries: Stealing Power for Themselves: If the players refuse to work for either mage, they may seek to unlock the Orb's true potential and restore its gems—but this risks drawing the attention of Zuggtmoy, Iuz, or even the Elder Elemental God.

entries.name: Act 2: The Hunt for the Elemental Gems
entries: If the players or Senshock seek to fully empower the Orb, they must venture into the Elemental Nodes to recover the missing gems.

entries.name: The Elemental Nodes (Each houses a Gem of Power)

items: Air Cavern: A storm-wracked maze of floating islands and lightning-wielding djinn.
Earth Burrows: A labyrinth of tunnels where sentient stone guardians hunt intruders.
Fire Pits: A volcanic battlefield where fire elementals and demons protect their domain.
Water Maze: A vast, drowned ruin where krakens and water spirits stalk those who dare enter.

entries.name: Challenges
entries: The players must face monstrous guardians, navigate deadly terrain, and outwit rival factions. Falrinth may try to manipulate them, secretly following their progress to claim the Orb for himself. Hedrack, suspecting treachery, may dispatch assassins to eliminate Senshock and his allies.

entries.name: Act 3: The Power Struggle
entries: Once the Orb is fully restored, the true battle for control of the Temple begins.

entries.name: 1. Senshock's Triumph
entries: If Senshock gains the Orb, he uses it to overthrow Hedrack, ascending as the new master of the Temple. The players become his enforcers, gaining dark rewards—but risk becoming pawns in a greater game of gods and demons. However, Iuz and Zuggtmoy will not tolerate Senshock's defiance, and vengeance will come.

entries.name: 2. Falrinth's Gambit
entries: If Falrinth claims the Orb, he betrays all sides and escapes into the shadows, seeking his own rise to power. The players may attempt to stop him, or he may manipulate them into fighting his enemies while he vanishes. If he escapes, Lolth gains a terrifying advantage in the war for the Temple.

entries.name: 3. The Players Keep the Orb
entries: If the players claim the Orb for themselves, Iuz and Zuggtmoy will know instantly. They must either master its dark power or seek the means to destroy it before it corrupts them. If they attempt to destroy it, they must follow the ancient ritual—but this risks collapsing the Temple and awakening something far worse.

entries.name: 4. The Orb is Destroyed
entries: If the players succeed in the destruction ritual, the Elemental Nodes collapse, and Zuggtmoy takes immense damage. The Temple is thrown into chaos, and its forces scatter. However, Hedrack and Iuz will hunt those responsible, ensuring that their war is not yet over.

entries.name: The Setup
entries: The adventurers find themselves in Nulb at a crucial moment. Rumors float through the grimy streets that Falrinth himself has been seen slipping through the shadows toward the Waterside Hostel. Dick Rentsch, the hostel's proprietor, is known to keep close ties with Romag and the Earth Temple—an unlikely ally for Falrinth, who owes fealty only to Lolth and himself. But alliances in Nulb shift faster than quicksand, rivals can find common ground when darker interests align.

items: {@b Negotiating Alliances:} Falrinth aims to quietly form a temporary alliance with Romag through Dick Rentsch, using their mutual distrust of Hedrack and Senshock to find common ground.
{@b Gathering Intelligence:} He seeks information about Madame Selentis and her growing influence in Nulb—especially her ties with Lucius Graeme and Hedrack's broader plans.
{@b Protecting the Orb:} Falrinth is wary of Hedrack's growing suspicion about his possession of the Orb of Golden Death. He needs allies and information to safeguard his power.

entries.name: Companions
entries: Falrinth is shadowed by two hardened brigands from Smigmal Redhand's personal enforcers. They're under strict orders to guard him and report his every move back to Smigmal, whose loyalty remains a volatile mixture of love and mistrust.

entries.name: Encounter at the Waterside
entries: The hostel's dim interior stinks of river mud and stale ale. Falrinth sits cloaked in shadows at a secluded table, his quasit familiar Kriitch invisible and lurking nearby. Dick Rentsch moves between tables, eyes flicking nervously toward the wizard as he quietly briefs his agents on Romag's behalf.

items: Watches newcomers warily, seeking potential threats or opportunities.
Listens attentively for any whispers concerning Lucius Graeme, Madame Selentis, or Supreme Commander Hedrack.
Engages Dick in brief, intense conversations that always conclude abruptly.
{@b Insight (DC 17):} Adventurers notice Falrinth's wary demeanor, his fingers subtly weaving arcane patterns beneath the table.
{@b Arcana (DC 15):} Characters sense magical protections woven around Falrinth, as well as the unseen presence of his quasit familiar.
{@b Perception (DC 18):} Detect the nervous behavior of Dick Rentsch and his men, revealing their discomfort in dealing with Falrinth openly.
{@b Non-engagement:} Prefers to avoid direct conflict unless cornered or openly provoked. He will gather information and retreat quickly back to his lair beneath the Temple.

{@b Combat and Escape:} If confronted, Falrinth deploys illusions, confusion, and misdirection. Kriitch, his quasit, attacks from stealth to distract and confuse while his brigands engage physically, allowing Falrinth an opportunity to slip away.
{@b Intel on Hedrack and Orb:} Information overheard or forcibly extracted can reveal Falrinth's possession of the Orb of Golden Death and his deeper connections to Lolth and Smigmal Redhand.
{@b Insight on Madame Selentis:} Information gathered exposes Madame Selentis's critical role as Hedrack's hidden strategist in Nulb, potentially weakening Hedrack's position if leveraged correctly.
{@b Alliance Disruption:} Falrinth's intentions to align temporarily with Romag through Dick Rentsch can disrupt the delicate power balances of the temples.

entries.name: Intelligence Gained
entries: Confirmation of Falrinth's dangerous dual loyalties—his true fealty to Lolth, and his willingness to undermine Hedrack's authority within the Temple. Awareness of the growing tension between temple factions, particularly Romag's Earth Temple and Hedrack.

entries.name: Falrinth's Journal
entries: Unravel the schemes of the rogue wizard Falrinth and prevent the full restoration of the Orb of Golden Death before Zuggtmoy can be freed.

entries.name: Introduction
entries: While exploring the Temple of Elemental Evil, the adventurers discover Falrinth's journal, a tattered tome containing cryptic writings about his obsessive pursuit of the Orb of Golden Death (Yellowskull). The journal reveals that Falrinth has already obtained the gemless Orb and is now hunting for the four Elemental Power Gems, which are hidden in the Elemental Nodes.

If he succeeds in reuniting the Orb with its lost gems, he will unlock its full power and break the final seals on Zuggtmoy's prison, allowing the Demoness of Fungi to be fully unleashed upon the world once more.

"At last, the golden skull is mine. Its power thrums beneath my fingers, but it is incomplete. The fool priests kept it as a hollow relic, unaware of the true strength hidden within. Without its gems, it is but a gilded shell—useless to Zuggtmoy's cause. I must recover the missing pieces, buried deep within the Elemental Nodes."

items: Falrinth has already stolen the Orb of Golden Death from the Temple's hidden vaults.
He understands the Orb is powerless without the four Elemental Power Gems.
He knows the gems are inside the Elemental Nodes and is searching for them.

entries: "The first gem has been found—a gift of the earth, pulsing with its deep power. When I placed it within the Orb's diadem, the wards shuddered, as if in recognition. A single crack in the prison's foundation. The process works! Three more gems remain, and then the final ward shall fall.

items: Falrinth has obtained the Earth Gem and placed it into the Orb.
The act of restoring one gem has already weakened Zuggtmoy's prison.
Falrinth is emboldened, believing he can break the final wards by restoring all four gems.

entries: "The fools see only a gilded trinket, a relic of a forgotten war. But I know the truth—the Orb of Golden Death is the key, the heart of the Temple's power! With its lost gems restored, the very foundations of the world will quake, and Zuggtmoy shall walk free once more!"

"It was no simple task, but I have breached the threshold of the Nodes. The Air Caverns were first—howling winds and screaming spirits, but the gem eluded me. My minions scoured the Burrows, yet the Earth Gem alone will not suffice. I must press deeper. The Fire Pits and Water Maze remain unexplored. Somewhere within these wretched realms, the remaining gems wait for me."

items: Falrinth has entered the Elemental Nodes using the power of the Orb.
He has searched the Air and Earth Nodes but has not yet found all four gems.
He is preparing to explore the Fire Pits and Water Maze next.

entries: "Fools! They do not understand the forces at work here! The final ward is bound to Zuggtmoy herself—only she may remove it. But I have found the means to hasten her return! I have traveled deep into the Nodes and given her the last gem. The Orb is whole again! Soon, she will open the final door with her own hand, and the world will tremble at her rebirth!"

items: Zuggtmoy must open the final ward herself—Falrinth cannot do it alone.
Falrinth has delivered the last Elemental Power Gem to Zuggtmoy inside the Elemental Nodes.
Time is critical, as Falrinth's actions have set the stage for Zuggtmoy's release.
{@b Recover the Orb of Golden Death:} The adventurers must retrieve the Orb from Falrinth before he fully restores it.
{@b Stop the Final Ward from Being Broken:} Zuggtmoy now has the final gem—the party must prevent her from using it to unlock her prison.
{@b Prevent Falrinth's Escape:} If he escapes, he will continue his mission elsewhere, possibly seeking allies among Iuz's forces.
{@b Destroy the Orb of Golden Death:} If all four gems are recovered, the party can attempt the ritual to destroy the Orb, though it requires extreme effort and knowledge.
{@b Disrupt Falrinth's Rituals:} By damaging the Elemental Nodes and disrupting their balance, the party can weaken the Orb's power, buying time.
{@b Learn the Truth About the Elemental Nodes:} Investigate their origins and how they are tied to Zuggtmoy's influence.

entries.name: Disrupting Deggum's Alliance - the Blackthorn Tribe
entries: The adventurers receive troubling reports of a growing alliance between the Temple of Elemental Evil and the monstrous Blackthorn Tribe. Commander Deggum, the Temple's security chief, has established ties with Chieftain Garghuk of the Blackthorn Tribe, an ogre-dominated warband in the eastern Gnarley Forest. Intelligence suggests Deggum is using the Blackthorn ogres to bolster the Temple's defenses and prepare for an impending assault on Verbobonc's borderlands. If left unchecked, this could lead to widespread devastation.
The adventurers must infiltrate the Temple, sever Deggum's alliance with the Blackthorn Tribe, and prevent a full-scale invasion.

items: Disrupt Deggum's Alliance with the Blackthorn Tribe: Undermine the growing partnership between the Temple and the monstrous warband.
Steal the Black Scarab Amulet: This magical sigil grants Deggum the authority to command the Blackthorn warriors. Without it, his influence will be significantly diminished.
Infiltrate Deggum's Command Post: Gather intelligence on the Temple's recruitment efforts, troop movements, and invasion plans.
Sow Discord Among the Ogre Ranks: Manipulate the Blackthorn Tribe into turning against Deggum and the Temple.
Discover Deggum's Secret Plans: Unearth details of a larger military operation beyond publicly known.

entries.name: Deggum, Temple Recruiter
entries: Ambitious and cunning, Deggum views monstrous forces as key to the temple's dominance. He is a manipulator, using false promises and magical coercion to secure warriors. The Black Scarab Amulet is proof of his authority—without it, his influence wanes.

entries.name: Chieftain Garghuk, Ogre Warlord of Blackthorn
entries: A brutal but intelligent leader who wields immense control over his tribe. Wary of outsiders, but values strength and cunning above all else. A split within Blackthorn could make him doubt Deggum's leadership.

entries.name: Gloarok, the Enforcer (Ogre Gladiator, CE)
entries: Deggum's chief enforcer, tasked with keeping Blackthorn in line. Fears losing his position if Deggum falls from favor.

entries.name: Romag, Earth Temple Priest (Human Cleric, LN/LE)
entries: Resents Deggum's growing influence over the Temple's military forces. Could be persuaded to work against him for the right price.

entries.name: The Blackthorn Settlement
entries: Deep beneath the Gnarley Forest, the Blackthorn Caverns are an underground fortress of monstrous warriors. It is a place few outsiders have ever seen and lived to tell of.

items: Hidden Entrances: Accessed through a maze of forest tunnels and concealed paths.
Massive Underground Chambers: The settlement is self-sustaining, complete with crude forges, hunting parties, and fungal farms.
Slavery and Brutality: The duergar of the depths use Blackthorn as a slave trade hub, captured villagers are forced to work or fight in gladiatorial pits.

entries.name: Breaking Blackthorn's Hold
entries: Disrupting Deggum's alliance requires targeting their infrastructure:

items: Sabotage supply chains: Poison food stores or free captives to weaken morale.
Turn the ogres against Deggum: Convince Garghuk that he is being used as a pawn.
Assassinate key leaders: Gloarok's death could destabilize the entire operation.

entries.name: 1. The Blackthorn War Camp
entries: A forward base where warbands gather before raids. Guarded by orc skirmishers, gnoll scouts, and ogres. The party must infiltrate, gather intelligence, or sabotage their supplies.

entries.name: 2. Confronting Deggum
entries: His command post within the Temple is filled with guards and mercenaries. He wields dark magic and has an escape route prepared if the fight turns against him. He will retreat to Blackthorn if not dealt with quickly.

entries.name: 3. The Caverns of Blackthorn
entries: The party must navigate traps, tunnels, and monstrous lairs. The duergar might intervene, offering a deal or demanding a toll for passage. Garghuk may challenge the party to a test of strength instead of outright combat.

items: If the party fails, Blackthorn's warriors march against Verbobonc and the surrounding lands.
If Deggum escapes, he will seek vengeance, forming new alliances.
